boardgame: Arena: A Tavern Game Played with Dice and Cards
description:
Looking for an alternative to three-dragon ante? Arena is an easy to learn dice and cards game where players select terrible monsters and force them to face off against one another for gold and glory!
—description from the publisher

Arena is published as a print-and-play game requiring each player to have three standard dice.  The document includes thirty-six card images.

Arena fancifully is presented as a game that adventurers might play in the taverns of Forgotten Realms.

Players receive four coins (betting tokens), three dice, and are dealt four cards.  Each card denotes a "monster" that can be played into the Arena.

Play proceeds in rounds.  In each round, each player selects a card from their hand and plays it face-down on the table; all cards are revealed simultaneously.

Each "monster" has a specified number of dice (one to three) and players roll the appropriate number of dice to resolve the round - the high result wins, and ties are resolved by re-rolling.

The winning player selects one other player as the round loser.  The loser moves a coin (betting token) into the stakes and discards the losing card.  All other players return their played card to their hand.

Play proceeds until one player runs out of cards (and coins).  At this point the game winner is determined by the player holding the highest number of cards, and ties are resolved by summing the dice value of the held cards; if a tie still exists the players roll dice to determine a winner.  The winner claims the stakes.

—user summary

Mayor Snowflake has asked for help build up the town of ICEBURGH for the big winter sports expo planned next year! Luckily, some local friends can help!

Mr. Fox-It, Po & Lars Construction, Hoof's Roofs and The Iceburgh Sealers are ready to use their savvy building skills to turn ice and wood into new buildings to improve the town and show off the ICEBURGH to the world!

Welcome to Iceburgh is a 2-4 player resource management game where players gain water, wood and coins to upgrade buildings in the town of Iceburgh for victory points.

The game is made up of 15 iceberg tiles that are put into a 4 x 4 grid with 1 space left open.  A boat tile goes in that empty space and each player puts one of their work meeple onto the boat.

Over 12 rounds players will rotate as first player which allows them to move the boat either horizontally or vertically along the grid, shifting the icebergs when them move the boat.

Each iceberg has an action that a player can choose to take to help them gain or convert their resources as needed.  If a player has enough resources they may upgrade one of the current buildings on the icebergs to gain points. They do this on their turn by choosing the upgrade action or by upgrading a building adjacent to the boat by paying the appropriate cost (combination of water, wood and coins). Then the player will flip that iceberg over to show the upgraded building and new actions available.

The winner is the player at the end of 12 rounds with the most victory points and they will get the KEY TO ICEBURGH!
​
—description from the publisher

On Mount Circeo, which overlooks the beautiful Mediterranean Sea, stands an imposing and mysterious palace.
Since it is surrounded by a Mediterranean Maquis forest, arriving at the palace seems almost impossible, as if the forest plants themselves are moving to create everchanging paths to confuse and divert unwanted guests. But if you come from the sea and traverse the white beach below the promontory, where mysterious sea creatures are said to gather, the rumors say that a Passage to the fabulous mansion can be found.
It is time to put into practice your experience as explorers, to unveil the secrets of Mount Circeo and enter the palace and find out if the legends that say it is still inhabited by the mysterious sorceress Circe and her servants are true. Be careful, Scholars, because if you encounter the sorceress, you will have to resist her devious spells, capable of turning her enemies into servant animals for all eternity!

You are the ideal candidates for this job, so get going, and do not disappoint the expectations of Rocca Civetta. Set on the slopes of Mount Circeo, this Dungeonology expansion will lead you to unveil the secrets of the immortal sorceress Circe. You are going to face a thousand dangers: the sorceress will try to turn you into one of her sevant animals, her servants themselves will jealously guard the secrets of Circeo, the Leonine Guards of the palace will always be on your trail, and, if you aren't carefull, you may find yourself facing Circe's terrible pet Kraken!

—description from the publisher

Finally, there is the popular game of skill from the "Super Toy Club" for home! With a lot of team spirit and good communication, the players have to roll the ball from start to finish. Each player is responsible for one corner of the three-dimensional game board and only together can the goal be achieved. For this skill and concentration is required, because otherwise, the ball falls into one of the numerous holes. If that happens the team has to start again. But watch out, time is running fast and the other team is just waiting for their chance. In Labyrinthia, two teams play against each other. The team that works smarter and works better together will win in the end. Stops the time and challenges the opposing team: which team is faster?

A promo character for Adventure Island given out to Pegasus Supporters for Christmas 2018.

His stats are 3, 2, 3 but he lets everyone in his location roll 1 extra die for an action.

Serengeti is a fast-paced, cooperative board game packed with unique mechanics and beautifully designed artwork.  Players traverse treacherous terrain and navigate expansive grasslands in order to rescue endangered safari animals from the snare of trespassing poachers. Each player will take on the role of a reservation official. While on duty, these officials will take actions to save animals and arrest poachers. Players will have to evade stampedes, harsh weather, and poacher traps, but don't worry you will be given equipment to assist you in your efforts.

—description from the publisher

Scions of Avermhir (aver meer) is a new type of tactical strategy game that rewards thought out plans and maneuvers, without taking several hours to play! With a diverse set of goals to achieve and enhancements to acquire, you'll find that every game feels new and fun! The game welcomes new players and old hands alike, with each game ending down to the wire, not giving either player a chance to snowball out of control.

HOW DO YOU MAKE A GAME ABOUT THE GREAT CITY OF MONTEREY, CALIFORNIA? While it’s impossible to include everything, we jammed all the Monterey we could into this box. It’s full of outdoor adventures, family attractions, rich traditions and of course...one incredible bay - a little something for everyone! Looking for a fun way to experience California’s rugged coast?

HERE’S A QUICK IDEA OF HOW TO PLAY! Buy your favorite Monterey properties, increase your rent by collecting City Blocks and trading them in for Keys to the City. Sound easy? Well, add in some traffic jams, property tax and parking fines and it gets a little more difficult and a lot more fun! GET YOUR GAME ON!

So choose your token and advance to GO MONTEREY! You may soon be the Mayor… or out of the game for eating too many short rib grilled cheeses from The Cannery Row Brewing Company! THIS IS MONTEREY.... AND IT’S YOUR ROLL!

—description from the publisher
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
HOW DO YOU MAKE A GAME ABOUT THE GREAT CITY OF TALLAHASEE, FLORIDA? While it’s impossible to include everything, we jammed all the Tallahassee we could into this box. It’s full of outdoor adventures, canopy roads, deep-rooted culture, and of course…a whole lotta college spirit - a little something for every Tallahasseean! Solve the exercises first and get the card. Player with the most cards at the end of the game wins the game.

The boss has fired several employees and you will be doing extra hours. Unclogging the toilet is just one of these jobs, so let's see how we can take advantage of it.

This expansion for Lunes includes new reward objects, as well as a new competitive mod for 2 players on a single board.

—description from the publisher

Recorte de personal en la oficina: han echado a varios empleados y estaremos realizando trabajos que no nos corresponden. Destapar el baño es sólo uno de ellos, pero vamos a ver cómo le podremos sacar provecho.

Una expansión de Lunes que incluye nuevos objetos recompensa, además de una nueva modalidad competitiva para 2 jugadores en un solo tablero.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Each player receives a Finish card at the beginning of the game and tries to reach the race cars via tactical use of the dice. The race covers three laps. 
The player's Finish card shows in what order that player needs the cars to finish. For each correctly entered car, the player receives the specified score. The player with the highest score has won the game. If it's your turn, roll all four dice and distribute the numbers randomly to the participating cars, trying to achieve the result shown on your Finish card.

—user summary

A football game for 2 players, where each hexagon is a player of a team. The objective of the game is to score goals using your cunning and strategy.

This is a fast-paced game, pocket-sized, ideal in any occasion and playable in almost any environment.

The game contains 20 hexagons, 10 for each team in blue and red, and 1 wooden token representing the ball. Each character has special abilities, conditioning the way they move, or the way they are allowed to move the ball.

Some characters are able to move the ball long distances, others may jump above other players, there's even the option to climb on top of other players.. Come on ref! That's got to be a foul!


Es un juego de futbol para dos personas donde cada hexágono es un jugador del equipo. El objetivo es hacerle goles a tu contrincante con estrategias a corto plazo.

El juego es rapido, y pequeño (tamaño bolsillo) ideal para llevarlo a cualquier tipo de ocasión y jugar en espacios muy reducidos.

El juego consta de 20 Hexágonos, 10 del equipo azul y 10 del equipo rojo, y una ficha de madera que representará la pelota. Cada uno de los personajes tiene habilidades distintas, ya sea en la forma que se mueve el personaje (ósea el hexágono) o la forma en que mueven la pelota (la ficha).

Algunos tendrán la posibilidad de lanzar la pelota a distancia, otros saltar por encima de los demás jugadores, e incluso hay uno que tiene la opción de subirse encima de los otros jugadores para quitarles la pelota - "Eso es full referí!!".

In Death Rabbits you fight against other clans of mutant rabbits, in Battle Royale style, with the goal to destroy them and conquer the post-apocalyptic world!

Death Rabbits draws influence from old style turn-based artillery games such as Scorched Earth and Worms, incorporating move, attack and defend mechanisms along with concepts such as Range, Line of Sight and Area of Effect.

The game is played on a landscape made up of 6, out of 8 tiles, randomly selected.  The Rabbits then move across the landscape trying to kill each other, whilst defending themselves against incoming attacks from other clans.

Players must manage their arsenal of Explodey, Stabby and Shooty weapons to inflict the maximum damage on their rival clans.  But, they must be aware of their opponents' weapons and build their defences appropriately.

Death Rabbits also contains 5 Booster cards which come into play later in the game, giving the clans additional powers that enhance their attack, defence or movement abilities.

The last clan with Rabbits alive rules the world!

—description from the designer

From the Cellar: Pack 9 is an Advanced Squad Lead scenario pack released by Le Franc Tireur in 2019. It’s a peculiar product, collecting scenarios from the French Vae Victis magazine, seven other original scenario designs, as well as a small booklet with some personal notes, two articles and a comic.

From the Cellar: Pack 9 also comes with the Italian nationality chart, which should originally have been published with Le Franc Tireur #14: Italians[/thing]. On the back of this chart is an original map with OB information, on which the reader may track an AAR printed as one of the articles. The article is the creative AAR of a game where only twos and sevens are rolled. The other article is a story about a Ukrainian unit that was well researched.

The scenarios are:

FT273: Former Foes: Xuan Loc, Northeast of Saigon, Indochina, 29 October 1945: Viet Minh versus Indian Infantry and Elements of the IJA Southern Expedition Army: Designed by Jean-Pascal Paoli
FT272: Rise of the Viet Minh: Dong Mu, Indochina, 10 April 1945: Viet Minh versus French: Designed by Laurent Closier
FT271: The Lock of Colmar: Kayserberg, France, 18 December 1944: Americans and Foreign Legion versus Germans: Designed by Lionel Colin
FT270: Revenges at Saint-Julien: Saint-Julien, France, 9 August 1944: French Partisans versus Germans
FT269: End of the Rope: Stanly Mound, Hong-Kong, 23 December 1941: Japanese versus Commonwealth: Designed by Philippe Naud
FT268: Spain’s Crusaders: Sitno, Russia, 23 October 1941: Spanish Azul versus Russians
FT267: Thugny-Trugny: Thugny-Trugny, France, 9 June 1940: French versus Germans: Designed by Jim Suderman
FT266 (VV75): A Fine Mess: Pommereuil, France, 18 May 1940: German versus French: Designed by Philippe Naud
FT265 (VV73): Balkan-Kessel: Bolec, Serbia, 17 October 1944: Germans versus Partisans and Russians: Designed by Philippe Naud
FT264 (VV62): Hand off the Loot: Near Paulhac, France, 2 June 1944: French Partisans versus Germans: Designed by Philippe Naud

FT262 (VV41): 20 Years Later: Berensdorf, Germany, 8 September 1939: Germans versus French: Designed by Philippe Naud

Zurvivors is a card game that will challenge your fears and put you in the middle of a city taken by the zombie apocalypse.

Your goal as a survivor is to flee through the city streets until you reach the rescue helicopter within 6 hours, but if you are caught by the zombie horde, you should help your new comrades exterminate the human race.

To advance on city streets, survivors must reveal cards to compose one of 3 personal goals, giving advantage to those who complete first.

Various supplies can be found on city streets and stored in your backpack if you can get them.

Each survivor has a different ability that can be activated once per round, making the match even more exciting.

If some unarmed survivor meets the horde wandering the street, that's not a good sign ... for your friends.

You will become a zombie of the horde that will play in favor of eliminating the survivors!

Would you and your friends survive for 6 hours?
Time is decisive, so hurry! Before it's too late.

Bike races for 2 - 6 players, with dice throws and card collecting.

Each player has three riders at the start. To start, you need to roll a six. Before the dice roll, the player must indicate one of his three riders for whom the roll is used.

The route is divided into three sections and there are different colored event fields.

Opposing riders can be thrown out. But not in all fields.
There are the following fields:
3 start fields
a target field
white squares (you can hit opposing riders)

Black, red, yellow squares: here the player will be asked questions of the corresponding color, depending on the color field. The question is the player sitting behind the player. If the question is answered correctly, the player gets the card, he has not given a correct answer, then the questioner may keep the card.

Blue fields: these fields serve the catering of the drivers, here can not be beaten. The VIP fields, before leaving you can call according to "VIP-VIP" and then die, the number is then doubled. However, if you only roll a 1, you have to go back to the last stage start.

Pink fields: are event fields, the texts are on the plan.
At the end of the racetrack, at the third stage you can not leave the time loop until you have collected 15 question cards, otherwise you have to go in a circle until you have done that, then you can leave the loop and drive to the finish. Who is the best cyclist?

—user summary

The board shows two circles on which one can carry out his activities. There are lots of action fields. The task of the players is to put together ideas for inventions and to apply for a patent in Germany or in Europe.
With patent sale or with licensing you make the big money. And with a clever game play, you can win as the richest player.
from inventor to millionaire.

—user summary

Die DM rollt (1st game) 
The four caps of each player start on the spaces marked with a "S". Movement is determined by two dice. The player choses the result of one die to move a cap. If a cap gets on a space of another color with a number, the player gets the corresponding amount of game money from the player of this color. The other player may avoid this with a succesfull counter move. He rolls a die. If he manages to reach the cap of his opponent, the opponent has to pay and his cap is returned to a starting position.
If a player traps an opponent cap on a space without a number, he takes the trapped cap with him when he moves again. If this pair of caps get on a numberef space of the color of the trapped cap, the trapped player gets no counter move.
The game ends, when one player is broke. The player with the most money wins the game.

Money Maker (2nd game)
In this game movement is not decided by dice, but by a deck of Cards. Each player sets his 4 caps on numbered spaces of another color. Then each player draws three cards of his color. If there are caps on the spaces indicated by the cards the player has to pay his opponents the indicated amount of money. The owners of caps on spaces of his color not indicated by the cards have to pay hin instead. The game ends afer the whole talons have been played an agreed upon number of times. The player with the most money wins the game.

—user summary

Based off the Mobile game Battle Bears!

Have you and up to 5 friends ever crash landed on a hostile planet pursued by a horde of explosive zombie teddy bears and only enough supplies remaining for one of you to make it back off the planet? Have you had to Fight to the Death using your own unique abilities, and with the random weapons and equipment you can find to be the Last One Standing? After dying, have you then come back as a cute zombie teddy bear and attempted to revenge kill your opponents?

Oh, you haven't? Well why not? It's about time you had the experience. BattleBears is coming to your tabletop!

In Battle Bears: The Board Game the goal is to be the last bear standing! Each player takes two actions on their turn. You can shoot, run around picking up items, use a piece of gear or your special ability! At the end of your turn place a huggable swarm to try and trap your enemies. If you die, don’t worry - death isn’t the end! You come back as an even stronger, ultimate huggable set on taking revenge on the ones who destroyed you.

boardgame: Showdown Battle:  Marianas and Philippine Sea Jun44
description:
Operational level game of the landings and any accompanying air and naval battle(s).  Individual fleet and light carriers and battleships - both Yamato and Musashi sortied in this - 2-ship cruiser and 4-ship destroyer units (mostly), Guam and Saipan invasion forces, and implicit submarines. Divebomber, torpedo bomber, and Japanese Betty medium bomber squadrons - fighters are also implicit (in the game system) for playability.
100 mile/160 kilometer hexes and 3-hour turns over a possible 6 days.  As for a good Leyte Gulf game, there could be one USN player in command of Task Force 52's landings, old battleships, and taffies (baby flattop groups) and one for modern battleship and fast carrier TF58 ... with coordinating communications possibly delayed? }:)
Secret *Japanese* strategic intelligence roll, with a 17% chance of them already near the Marianas and an also un-historical 33% chance of them entering the west side of the chartboard at the same time the Americans enter the east side - or they are delayed a full day, as historically.  Dummy units and detection rules for operational Fog of War.  My abstract "betting" ground combat system.

Game balance victory point compensation.  :)

—description from the designer

Object of the game: 
To dislodge opponents clip on heart with a pliable plastic sword.

Before Starting Play:
Each player takes one sword and one Heart that he clips on his person, below his shoulders or above his knees.
The Heart may not be placed on the arms. Any player then takes the arrows and throws them in the air or places them in a circle radius of about six feet , disregarding where the colors may fall. The spinner is then placed in the center of the circle.

How to play:
Each player stands on the outside pattern of the arrows . The first player then spins the dial . The dial stops on a blue arrow , the players quickly place any foot on any convenient blue arrow , with toes pointing in the direction of the arrow. When the spinner shows geeen players place the other foot on any green arrow . As the game continues , players may place either foot on the arrow that is most convenient to them . When the spinner stops on a Heart, the player calls out “HAVE A HEART” and each player tries to get each other’s heart with his sword. The hearts may be removed by either knocking them off or by placing the curved part of the sword thru the hole in the heart and pulling it off. If a player loses balance and falls or his heart is removed , he is eliminated from the game. The last player remaining is the winner.

—user summary

description:
Fly Fight Win: Warbirds is a hex-based, miniatures wargame rules set for aerial combat in World War II. It emphasizes the importance of speed, altitude, and pilot skill, and minimizes much of the tedium typically associated with air-to-air combat games. It includes statistics for more than 60 frontline fighters of the era and a pilot advancement system for campaign games.

Warbirds is a standalone rules set and the first game in the Fly Fight Win series. All games in the series are designed for playability but retain sufficient detail to recreate historical outcomes (with a nod towards the physics of flight). Most scenarios can be played in under 60 minutes.

description:
This is an reduced map used for the 1822: Railways of Great Britain game, which is part of the 18xx series based on the growth of the Railways through the age of steam. It is based on a map of containing England, parts of Wales and Scottland, less of the north and southwest compared to the full 1822 map.

Players take the part of investors in Railway companies and then operate the companies to maximise their own wealth.

Three variants of the 1822 Medium Regional Scenario are given in the rules. The Starter version is intended for those with no experience with 1822. The Standard version is intended for players that wish to experience gameplay similar to the full 1822 game, but in a shorter duration. The Advanced version is more volatile and is the same as the previously web-published variant made available upon 1822’s release.

"The pack is back!"

If you were a child of the '90s, you'll love the nostalgia that The Fanny Pack Game from Buffalo Games evokes when it's part of your next game night. In this '90s-themed party game, each player channels one of eight '90s personas — from mall rat to grunge rocker — presented on the character cards. Each character card features a variety of items the player needs to collect to fill their fanny pack, like knee pads for the roller blader and a ring pop for the tween girl. Players trade cards to get the items they need, with each player aiming to be the first to fill their fanny pack. All pieces tuck nicely into the totally '90s-tastic neon fanny pack, and points are tracked using classic '90s milk caps.

Generate the track, choose your Action cards to advance, deal with the Dark Rider and the Events during the race. Will you reach the finish line… alive?

Play 4 game levels stand-alone or play them all in Campaign mode. Choose wether to compete against the other riders or to create alleances to fight the terrible Dark Rider, a crazy, armored motorcyclist that's hunting you with a rifle.
Ride is the board game inspired by the film that was produced by Lucky Red and Mercurious together with Tim Vision in association with Viris, directed by Jacopo Rondinelli, artistically supervisioned by Fabio&Fabio, written by Fabio Guaglione and Marco Sani.

In Ride, you are all expert riders doing downhill, a sport where you cycle at full speed following difficult tracks. The secret association Black Babylon has gathered you in an exclusive race but soon you all realize you are gambling your own life! To win the big money prize of this extreme reality show, you need to reach the end of the track scoring the most points and, most importantly, without dying.

User summary
ベストワードクラブ (Best Word Club) (Besutowādokurabu) is a Japanese word game.

Each round, one player is assigned as a judge. The judge reveals this round's subject which consists of an initial character card and an adjective card (for example, "a big thing that begins with あ"). Other players declare a word that suits the subject. The judge chooses the most suitable word, and the declarer of that word scores a point.

Play several rounds and the player with the most points is the winner.

The designer says "Best Word Club is a word-game. It asks some questions to the players. For example, "What is the strongest word starting with M?"
The players except the king answers, "Minitour" "MechaGodzilla" "Music" "Money!" "Me!" and so on...

Then a king chooese the best word from them. The player who says the best word gets a point! After everyone has been the king, the game is over. It's a very simle but deep word game."

Publisher's summary
2枚のカードによって頭文字と特徴が決まり、お題が提示されます。プレイヤーはそれに当てはまる言葉を1つずつ言います。判定人は、その中で1番お題にふさわしいものを選びます。選ばれた人は点数を得ます。

This game was awarded a gold medal in 1974 at the Geneva International Inventors Fair.

It is recommended for children from the age of 12 to playfully learn how important it is to have a good education and vocational training to succeed in life.

The first part of the game is therefore about school and work.
In the second part, players try to buy as many businesses and stocks as possible, hiring employees and working for profit.

In this game, you will learn, among other things, about loans and repayments, corporations, business companies, pre-emption rights, payment difficulties and bankruptcy.

All things that are unfortunately not explained in school lessons.

The Achean Alchemists, with their unparalleled knowledge of herbology, travel far and wide across Mor Nadar to gather the herbs and plants they need to brew powerful potions.  The novices amongst the Acheans are known as Gardeners as they tend the Great Gardens of Achea.

This pack give you a first glimpse of resource gathering and item creation in Legends Untold. Gather Herbs and Brew Potions on your adventures as you seek entry to the great city of Dun Mordhain.

BloodRunes is a game of Strategy and Chance.  Collect Runes in your hand to cast your spells and gain Blood Fortunes.  Steal from other Casters or cast Protection Wards to defend against attack.  All Players share the same board and must cast Runes that benefit themselves without helping their opponents.  Keep a close eye on other Casters' spells to determine their strategies, while bringing your own plans to fruition.  Use your Acolytes to do your bidding and win the game.  Or scheme with other Casters to target a particular enemy.

CAUTION: BloodRunes will start fights and break up marriages.  Play with caution.

BloodRunes is a 2 to 4 player game and takes about an hour to play.  Great for a mind-bending afternoon of gaming.

You are a talented painter. At least that’s what your mom says. Your art teachers, however, say you’re good… ish. But somehow, you still manage to get portrait commissions (mostly friends and family). They don’t pay very well (just in Exposure), but it’s still nice to be appreciated. So get out there and paint!

In Art-ish you’ll be mixing colors to paint splatters on your canvas with the goal of fulfilling commissions. Be the first player to earn 8 Exposure points to win.

On their turn, players create Splatters using different combinations of Paint Tubes from their hand with the goal of reproducing the model on one of the Commission Cards.

When a player has fulfilled a Commission card by matching its Splatters in the proper location, they gain that number of Exposure and a special ability to use during their subsequent turns. Abilities and points vary depending on the difficulty of the Commission.

-description from publisher

Circus Corgis is a Trick-Taking, Treat eating, Star Bidding, low rider game for all levels! 
Once cards are dealt, players bid on how many tricks they’ll win. The more specific you are with your prediction, the greater the reward. Earn Treats to incentivize your pups and play the higher value of a card. Earn 5 Trophies to win!! 
Outbid and out-trick your opponents, and put on the most spectacular show in Circus Corgis!

Players are dealt 9 cards, then bid on how many Stars (or tricks) they will win.  
In turn order, each player plays a corgi. They may play a Treat to play that card's higher value. Players must follow suit and play the same color as the starting card. When all players have played a card, the highest card wins a star. Play continues until all cards have been played. 
When the round is finished, each player assesses if they have successfully achieved their bid. If so, they take the corresponding rewards: treats and trophies. If they over bid or under bid, they do not receive any points. 
The game ends when one player earns a total of 5 points!

—description from the publisher

description:
Catastrophe! The Grand Museum of Art may have to close forever! The Museum Director just jumped ship, leaving behind an enormous hidden deficit.

It’s up to you and your fellow curators to increase visitor attendance and keep the museum running. To do this, you must present grandiose exhibitions that will attract crowds of visitors and fill the museum coffers to save the institution from financial ruin.

The curator with the most popular exhibitions will become the new Museum Director!

In the beginning every player has got a couple of art (painting) cards and also a couple of (secret) exhibition cards as goals to complete collections. Every exhibition card consist of art cards, but there are a lot of paintings that belong to more exhibitions. If a player collects every card of her/his exhibition card she/he gets more victory points than the independent cards have. 
To collect cards players have to roll dice and move pawns, they can get cards (art or exhibition) in various forms, they can steel from each other, they can answer trivia about the paintings. At the end of the game the player with the most victory point wins the game.

description:
In Rainbow Cats, players compete to claim as much space in the house as possible for their favorite cat while making sure their least favorite cat's objects are nowhere to be seen.

At the start of play, players draw two cat cards to establish their favorite and least favorite cats, information they need to keep secret! If a player somehow draws the same cat for both their favorite and least favorite cats, that player will not have a least favorite cat. The house consists of a 5x5 grid made up of items from seven different cats, each representing a color of the rainbow.

The Seven Cats:
Phoenix
Ginger
Daisy
Jade
Azul      
Iris      
Violet

Contents: 
14 Cat Identity Cards
140 Cat Item Cards
9 Grid Goal Cards
25 Starting Grid Cards
Last Played Card Token

There are 7 cats total and 20 item cards for each specific cat within the item deck. During game play, players will take turns placing item cards on  a 5 x 5 grid. Players will also have  secret guide showing anywhere from 1-5 points for each spot on the grid. The player earns points for every preferred item card placed and loses points when their least favorite cat’s items are on the grid. Each player has a different guide, meaning each player can gain or lose points for different spots.  The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Types of Cards:
Cat Identity: Each player gets 2 of these. Keep them secret. 
Grid Goal: Each player gets 1 of these. Also keep this secret.
Cat Items: Players will get a lot of these throughout the game.

Set Up: 
To start the game , first distribute the starting grid cards face up on the table, revealing 25 colorful spaces where players can play item cards. Then shuffle and give each player 2 Cat Identity cards, 1 Grid Goal card, and 5 Item cards. Play will start with the person who has the most cats. If there is a tie then rock paper scissors should suffice. Players DO NOT select a favorite and least favorite cat at this time.

During the beginning stages of the game, players will take turns drawing 1 item from the items deck and playing 1 item within the 5 x 5 grid, not placing any cards on top of existing Item cards within the grid. 
They also must place cards at least 2 spaces from the last card played if able.

When they place an Item card on the grid, that player will also place the token to show the last played Item card. If there are no spaces available 2 spaces from the last card played then place anywhere an Item card has not been placed. Once 25 item cards are placed on top of all starting grid cards, players must select their favorite cats by placing it face down in front of them and adjacent to the 5 x 5 grid. The cat card left in their hand is their least favorite cat. Remember that if both are the same then they do not have a least favorite cat.

How to play: 
At this time shuffle the remaining item deck and deal each player 10 additional item cards in order to build individual decks. From this point forward, players will draw and play from their individual decks. Players will continue to draw 1 item card and place 1 item card on top of existing item cards until all players have no cards left in their individual item decks. There is no limit to the number of cards that can be stacked on top of other cards.

Don’t forget: Players can only place item cards 2 or more spaces from the last played item card.

End of Game: 
After all item cards have been played from the players’ individual decks, players will reveal their favorite cats and calculate their points based on their secret grid card, giving positive points for each item of the cat on the grid. Players will then reveal their least favorite cat and referring to their goal grid card, deduct points from their total for each item on the grid from their least favorite cat. The player with the highest total points wins.

Single Player Variant:
In the single player variant of Rainbow Cats, you will shuffle all 140 cards and make a 5 x 5 grid with 5 item cards in each space. The remaining cards will become your deck. You will then shuffle your deck and deal yourself 5 cards. Next, you will shuffle and deal yourself two cat cards.

Unlike the multi-player version, you cannot have the same favorite and least favorite cat and will need to draw another cat card.

The goal of this game is to remove all 20 items for the favorite cat. You remove 2 items from anywhere on the grid as long as they belong to same cat. The only items you cannot remove are from your least favorite cat. You can also peak at as many item cards on the grid as you’d like to help plan where you want  which cards to move. You can however move any item card from 1 space to another when you collect 2 of your favorite cat’s items.

For example, if Phoenix is your favorite cat and there are 2 red items for Phoenix showing, you will remove those 2 items and the items make a pair. When you remove a pair of items from your favorite cat, you can also move . Cards in hand can also be used. When cards in hand are played, it is replaced with a card from the remaining deck. The goal is to get all 20 items for your favorite cat.

It is a dark time for the inhabitants of the fantastic world of Bizmia. A period of desolation.

Bizmuth sites that had been used for thousands of years as a source of life and power, just went dark all at once. Their glow has now faded and their protective magic is no more.

Possessed creatures are terrifying everyone that dared to stay behind. Only those with proper equipment could defend themselves.

Catastrophic distortions in energy are also occurring everywhere without explanation. Anything effective or even a little bit useful against the forces of evil seems to have teleported to unknown locations. Will you be able to retrieve them?

You will venture in the world of Bizmia to look for your Gear Set as one of the four Heroes; Alf the Marksman, Orora the Warrior, Cosimo the Mage, Abinacle the Sorcerer. Your only fighting chance is to use your set’s unique abilities and pray for the support of the Spirit Dice.

Will you be the one Hero to restore the balance of Bizmia by the End of the last Cycle ?

Will you be able to finaly liberate The Dragle from his curse ?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ivar the Boneless: The Northumbrian Blood-Eagle Campaign, 866
description:
1st of November 866AD, All Saints Day in the Northumbrian City of Eoforwic (Yorvik in Danish), would have been a hive of activity as the good folk went about their lives drawn to the Mynster for the feast of All the Saints and the last market fair before winter. Little did they realize the great calamity about to enter their lives, as outside the gates was a wolf, Ivar the Boneless (son of Ragnar). Standing over seven foot tall, a great warrior and military tactician but also a tyrant, Ivar was the cruel and ruthless leader of the mycel heathern here, or Great Heathen Army.

Ivar, with his brothers Ubbe & Halfdan, along with their army arrived from Ireland years earlier. After landing in East Anglia in 865 AD they demanded and received Danegeld in the form of horses and supplies from King Edmund and the East Angles. The Vikings now had mobility by land and sea. Their target was Northumbria, and the domination of this northern kingdom with its superior river and road networks that linked Yorvic and Dublin to the Scandinavian homeland.

Northumbria in 866 was in a state of savage civil war. Osberht, the rightful King of Northumbria, had been deposed by Aella and a battle for control of Northumbria raged. Into this vacuum entered Ivar and his army. Chaos reigned as Ivar and the Viking Hordes began to launch attacks throughout Deira, looting, pillaging and killing in what could only have been a strategic plan by Ivar to weaken, subdue, cower, and splinter his enemies.

By March of 866 The rival Northumbrian Kings had reached a tacit agreement to stop their civil war to march on the Vikings and take back their kingdom. Although weakened by civil war, it also meant that the warriors of these Saxon lands were battle hardened, well trained and in such numbers that a full frontal assault against the Vikings at Yorvic was planned. For a short period it looked like Saxon success was theirs’ for the taking. But Ivar had arranged his defenses with all the cunning of an Alpha Wolf and the Saxons were defeated and King Alle would suffer the gruesome Ritual of the Blood Eagle.

Ivar the Boneless is as much a strategic as tactical game. While there will be pitched battles both players will have to make decisions as to when and where to fight, and how best to maximize your strengths, whether Viking or Saxon.

—description from the publisher

description:
Macau-Wars Variation

Macau Wars It’s a major game variation of the Macau card game based on the Macau (card game) Macau Plawno Variation, still being in development on github.

Gameplay

2 or more players are dealt 5 cards each; after that, the top card from the deck is then placed on the table. Play starts to the dealer's right.

The next card played must be of the same suit or the same value as the card on the table. For example, If a 7 of spades was on the table, the player can play a 9 of spades or a 7 of clubs or any other card that matches 7 or spades. If the player cannot play a card, he must draw from the deck.

Cards cannot be played in runs, i.e. 5 of Spades, 6 of Spades, 7 of Spades, 7 of Hearts, 8 of Hearts, 8 of Clubs, 7 of Clubs but a minimum of two or more the same value cards can be played.

When an action card is played, the player next (or previous depend on the action card) in the sequence must complete the action or add to it to pass it onto the next player in sequence. When down to a single card, a player must say "Macau!". If a player forgets to call "Macau" before the next players move, the player must draw 5 cards. The winner of the game is the first player to have no cards; in instances of more than 2 players, gameplay continues until only one player is left with cards. In case of Macau Championships once the first player to have no cards wins and the game stops. The winner shuffles the cards and the new game starts. One point is then assigned to the winner.

For the avoidance of doubt: If the player plays King of Spades and King of Hearts with King of Hearts on top the next player will pick up 5 cards as the top card takes precedence in this case.

War

In this game, a scenario called a war and deception can be created when players consecutively play 'special cards' to make other players increase their card count.

War is started when 2, 3, King of Spades (to a previous player) or King of Hearts is played. Causing players to pick up cards. When a war starts, players can play any 2s and 3s as the suit and value are being ignored during the war. Players can also play King of Spades and King of Hearts regardless of the suit currently on the deck and value of the cards as long as they are 2, 3 or mentioned Kings. Queen of Hearts can be played anytime during the war to stop the war causing the player to not pick up any cards. In this variation of the game, players can often pick up as many as 20 cards (they all add up, e.g. 2+3+2+King of Spades+King of Heart=17).

When King of Spades is played the previous player must pick up 5 cards or play another action card (any 2, 3, King of Hearts, King of Spades (to a previous player)). After the previous player, the game goes back to the player who played King of Spades.

Any queen can be placed at any time on any war card regardless of the current suit. See below section for the action of each Queen.

Action Cards

2, 3, King of Spades, King of Hearts - War cards that cause players to pick up cards (2,3,5 respectively).

If a Jack or Ace is played, the player placing the Jack or Ace can call for a non-action card value (in case of Jack) or a suit (in case of Ace). The difference is that the suit is ignored and any Jack can be placed on any Ace, and any Ace can be placed on any Jack, therefore, ignoring the requested suit or value of the previous player.

Queen of hearts - stops the war and causes the player not to pick up any cards.

Queen of diamonds - the next person is skipped (also during the war).

Queen of spades - order of play switches directions (also during the war).

Queen of clubs - player can chose who play next (cannot chose her/himself) (also during the war).

If a 4 is played the deception part starts, and all the players must miss a go, unless they have a 4 (if they don't have a 4 or they chose not to play the 4, they don't pick a card from the deck), in which case they can add this to the original 4, and all players in sequence must miss a go, unless someone has a 4, in which case they can add this to the original 4 and so on. If no one else has a 4 then the last player, who put 4 plays as many times as there are 4s. For example, if there are four 4s after the last player played the 4, the player must play four times. Note: the last player will play four times against him/herself, that also include all the action cards which will apply to the player him/herself. The deception is when someone doesn't want to play a 4 but has it and can play a 4 after everyone said they don't/have a 4 causing this player to have more turns.

Joker replaces any card.

Open Cards

The more difficult version of Macau Wars (and any Macau variation) is a game played with open cards. That means that all the players cards and the draw deck is shown. This is similar to Chess where by knowing all the cards players must think about strategy and future moves. Open cards variation might take hours to finish.
Hyde Society is a fantastical game set in the Victorian era, with a traitor mechanism reminiscent of the one in Betrayal at House on the Hill.

boardgame: Legends Untold: The Druid Novice Booster
description:
The Druiach Path guides those who seek to become one with nature upon Mor Nadar. It allows the Neophytes that follow its ways to draw on the powers of the animals, insects and reptiles found deep within the Weeping Caves and Great Sewers of Dun Mordhain.

The Druid Pack gives you the first steps along the path to become a true Druid of Mor Nadar.  Gain a companion and make use of the fauna within the environment to succeed on your adventures with Legends Untold.

Four extra cards are attached to the back of the Druid Booster to as errated cards.

Foes! Loot! Strange wells! No Adventure is complete without finding Loot guarded by the strange and unusual inhabitants of the Great Sewers and Weeping Caves.  Plumb the depths of the Sewers and Caves to find the Great Diversion to see where your Adventure may continue.

The Treasure Pack gives more Foes and Loot to switch into any adventure in Legends Untold.  The Great Diversion is a unique Location that links together the Caves and Sewers and allows you to continue onto the Ancient Crypts and Brethren Works.

Princeton 1777 is a Tactical level board-wargame on the Battle of Princeton, 3 January 1777. It is essentially a quasi-designer's kit. The rules are 'complete'; however, if you want to play the game you will have to make your own map and counters from the facsimiles provided. Units are either infantry, cavalry, artillery, or headquarters. Units range in size from company to battalion/regiment; artillery units are batteries. Rules were typed and mimeographed.

—user summary
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
publishers: The Avalon Hill Intercontinental Kriegspiel Society

description: 1863:  Trans-Continental Railroads is part of the 18XX train game / stock company family of games.  It features the major (and private) joint-stock companies that competed to build the various trans-continental railways, and the regional companies that grew out of these railways.

An expansion for the fantasy card-drafting game Enchanters (or the stand-alone expansion, Enchanters: Overlords) featuring two brand new unique 25 card Kingdom Decks.

The Barbarians: The best defense is a good offense. Trade protection for power, accumulate as many attack points as you can, and challenge the fearsome Frost Dragon for victory.

The Faeries: These tricksters will reward you with great wealth and power.. but they'll reward your opponents as well. Balance what you gain against what you're forced to give up, use what you've won to defeat the capricious Faerie Dragon, and seize victory.

—description from the designer

Your goal in Three Magic, a.k.a. スリーマジック, is to collect three cards of mana (the power of the nature) in order to create a spell; create three spells to complete your magic. Do this first, and you win.
designers: Kei Tsukahara (つかはら けい)

description:
An expansion for the fantasy card-drafting game Enchanters (or the stand-alone expansion, Enchanters: Overlords) featuring two brand new unique 25 card Kingdom Decks.

The Angels: The blessings of the heavens descend in this kingdom deck. Heal your opponents for the power you need, risk ongoing wounds in exchange for the most powerful of equipment, and conquer the mighty White Dragons.

The Troglodytes: Nothing stays buried forever.  Fight powerful monsters that bury your equipment in their death throes, find more equipment, and do it again in order to take on the powerful Subterranean Dragon.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Call to Adventure: Kickstarter Exclusive Card Pack
description:
A set of six cards (3 heroes, 3 antiheroes) available to original backers of the Call to Adventure Kickstarter. These were packaged together but separate from the base game box.

Call of the Wild
1 Triumph
This turn, gain +1 to attempt a Triumph path, or +2 to attempt a Nature path

Word of Honor
1 Triumph
This turn, gain +1 to attempt a Triumph path, or +2 to attempt a Justice path

Leap of Faith
1 Triumph
This turn, gain +1 to attempt a Triumph path, or +2 to attempt a Divinity path

Necessary Evil
1 Tragedy
This turn, gain +1 to attempt a Tragedy path, or +2 to attempt a Villainy path

Occult Secret
1 Tragedy
This turn, gain +1 to attempt a Tragedy path, or +2 to attempt a Arcana path

Blind Ambition
1 Tragedy
This turn, gain +1 to attempt a Tragedy path, or +2 to attempt a Royalty path

This pack of 6 cards was also available as part of the Call to Adventure: Epic Origins Kickstarter campaign, where it was called the "Forgotten Feats" pack.

boardgame: Monsterpocalypse Miniatures Game: Destroyers Planet Eaters Monster – Rogzor
description:
Those who thought they had seen the worst the Planet Eaters had to offer when they had witnessed Gorghadra had yet to behold the terror brought by the one called Rogzor. Its footsteps have changed the course of rivers, and its raw power has leveled mountains. It cannot be reasoned with or understood and seems motivated solely by the desire to bring about the utter disintegration of the Earth and its inhabitants, siphoning power from everything it turns to ash. Woe to any man, machine, or beast that stands in Rogzor's path.

—description from the game box

boardgame: Monsterpocalypse Miniatures Game: Destroyers Lords of Cthul Monster – Yasheth
description:
The Lords of Cthul are enigmatic creatures of nightmare and madness, drawn from unknown nether regions to plague the world, aided by cultists who revere them like gods. Yasheth stands at a place of prominence in the blasphemous texts of the cults, an entity of negation and death, an invoker of antilife that transforms the world around it into whatever crawling horror the Cthul see as paradise. His shrieks alone have been known to rend the sanity from witnesses, and all living things quail and soon perish in his presence.

—description from the game box

boardgame: Fire of Eidolon: Beauko Ummbird Promo Card
description:
A promotional Hero for Fire of Eidolon available during the Overworld Kickstarter Campaign, featuring a character from the world of Overworld.

The promo card is an additional option for a player’s character from the start of the game.

Noble Steed: 0 AP (passive) When you begin your turn in the same chamber as another hero, you may choose to carry that hero with you when using MOVE actions until the end of your turn. Only one hero may be carried per turn.

Door Discovery (3 AP Once per Game): you may place a dungeon door meeple over any unconnected doorway at your current location and another over any other unconnected doorway on a different chamber tile, regardless of location. Any hero may MOVE between these two chamber whiles they remain in play, as if they were adjacent and connected.

boardgame: Monsterpocalypse Miniatures Game: Protectors Shadow Sun Syndicate Monster – Zor-Raiden
description:
Biomorphic and cybernetic nanotechnology empowers each of the zors, the mightiest class of the Shadow Sun Syndicate's living weapon-warriors. The soldier forming the basis for Zor-Raiden was specifically recruited as one of the last surviving members of a nearly forgotten ninja clan hidden away in the northeastern mountains. Considered brash and headstrong by ranking members of the Syndicate, Raiden is nonetheless extremely skilled in combat and has demonstrated uncanny unspoken coordination with other shinobi units.

—description from the game box

boardgame: Monsterpocalypse Miniatures Game: Protectors Terrasaurs Monster – Armodax
description:
Though the Terrasaurs have shown a remarkable willingness to fight alongside Earth's other defenders, some are trickier to work with than others. Armodax is a hulking monster covered in armored plates and boasting a weighty clubbed tail that serves better than any wrecking ball at toppling buildings. He has demonstrated unrestrained delight in annihilating skyscrapers, factories, and power plants while clashing with Earth's foes. Yet there is no question he is worth preserving, given his unstoppable ferocity. His allies have learned to ensure the complete evacuation of any zone he has entered.

—description from the game box

boardgame: Monsterpocalypse Miniatures Game: Building – Void Gate
description:
Where the Lords of Cthul stride into battle, reality warps and bends before tearing asunder. Void Gates are like wounds in the very fabric of the time-space continuum, and through them come otherworldly horrors to ravage the Earth. It is a top priority of all of Earth’s defenders to eradicate these unnatural structures as rapidly as possible.

—description from the publisher

During the 18th century the Island of Chiloé in Chile was dominated and governed by a group of sorcerers who called themselves "The Majority". This group of powerful sorcerers named Chiloé as "La Recta Provincia" (The Straight Province) which was made up of seven districts and under the control of an all-powerful King.
After the King's death the most influential wizards dispute the reign of "La Recta Provincia". However, only the one who gets the support of most districts will be crowned as the next monarch.

In this game you will embody a powerful sorcerer who, using economic, political and magical influence, must take possession of most of the districts in "La Recta Provincia". But beware, a dark character known as the "Trauco" can threaten your plans, unless you are the one who can use him as an ally.

Your goal is to gain control of four of the seven districts in the "Recta Provincia". Every player stars with only one die to acquire influence cubes (Economic, political or magical), you will use your cards to play those influence cubes on districts. When you meet the influence requirements to take control of a district you will also acquire its special die which will grant the controller with new powers (more influence, cards and other abilities) to continue taking control of other districts.

The initial die will also move the "Trauco", an automata character that will help the active player to acquire more influence, but can also shift the control of a district where both players have met the influence requirements.

Each player has a single die of their color and a hand of numbered cards. Each turn, all players roll their dice and then pick a card from their hand. Then each player gets to pick a die to use for scoring, with the lowest card going first. Your score is the value of your card multiplied by the value on the die, with a bonus if you get your own die.

In order to get the best die you'll have to use a low card... But if your card's too low then you can end up with fewer points than the person with the second lowest number! Can you outwith the other players for the high score?

—user summary

For some reason you find yourself in Evil Manhattan, about to be attacked by a bunch of assassins. Luckily the assassins run through the streets in a relatively predictable way - can you alter their route so you can escape alive?

Each round starts by laying out the city streets as a grid of cards and placing assassins at the top and players at the bottom. City cards have the same street layout on the front and back, but the front (face-down at the start) has weapons that can make assassins more dangerous. After the city and assassins are laid out, each player decides what part of their body to protect. Then each player in turn can place blocking tokens to block off streets. After that the players can choose which weapon to defend against, and then the cards that aren't blocked are flipped over and the assassins go to work!

The assassins follow the streets towards the players, but take a turn whenever possible, following the rules of the Japanese "amida kuji" lottery system. When an assassin reaches a player, damage is calculated based on the weapons the assassin picked up, their preferences, and what the player chose to block. Repeat this for three rounds and - if anyone survived that long - the player with the least damage is the winner!

—user summary

In 救ワレタガール ("Saved Girl"), you take on the role of a ghost trying to build up enough good deeds to get your wish granted. To build up good deeds you'll help troubled girls by possessing them to solve their problems.

Each girl has three attributes (Heart, Skill, Strength) and a point value. At the start of the game nine girls are played to the center of the table, and on each turn you can gather tokens or save a girl. To save a girl the power of the girl you currently posses has to equal or exceed the girl to be saved in all three attributes, but if it's too low you can make up the difference with tokens. You can also manipulate the girls currently available to be saved if none of them are a good match or you want to block your opponents.

After you've saved a girl, you always get to keep the points, but you can only possess one girl at a time for saving other girls, so you have to choose carefully. Also each player has one special ghost power they can use once per game, distributed secretly at the start.

—user summary

HOW DO YOU MAKE A GAME ABOUT THE GREAT CITY OF HOT SPRINGS, AR? While it’s impossible to include everything, we jammed all the Hot Springs we could into this box. It’s full of outdoor adventures, family attractions, rich traditions and of course...the birthplace of spring training baseball - a little something for everyone! Looking for a fun way to experience the Nation’s Health Resort?

HERE’S A QUICK IDEA OF HOW TO PLAY! Buy your favorite Hot Springs properties, increase your rent by collecting City Blocks and trading them in for Keys to the City. Sound easy? Well, add in some traffic jams, property tax and parking fines and it gets a little more difficult and a lot more fun! GET YOUR GAME ON!

So choose your token and advance to GO HOT SPRINGS! You may soon be the Mayor…or out of the game for eating too many tamales from McClard’s Bar-B-Q! THIS IS HOT SPRINGS.... AND IT’S YOUR ROLL!

—description from the publisher
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
5 card expansion for an extra challenge:

1 tiebreaker card
1 hyphen card
1 set of noun, verb and adjective cards



      Tiebreaker - The player that is in last place receives this card and will win ties.
     Hyphen - Add this to your games for a real challenge.
     Noun Verb Adjective -  Adds some optional restrictions and some press your luck. If your word fits the category in play, the value of the word goes up by 1. But you have to choose which suit when you write down your word.
