description: It's a Romanian board/card game in which you have to make your partner guess the name of the object that you have drawn from the pile.  All of the cards in the game represent objects, it has 1000 cards, and 172 of them are regional words specific to historical regions of Romania. The game takes place in three rounds: In the first round you can use as many words as you like,  in the second round you have to use only one word, and in the third round you have to mime the object or it’s use. Each player has 60 seconds to explain or guests as many cards as he/she can. The team that gathers as many cards as they can in all the three rounds wins the game.

During the game players play the parts of supporters and opponents of Polish-Lithuanian real union in the XVI century. Both sides aim to gather more votes on the Sejm in Lublin in order to force their ideals. The side which has more votes at the end of the game, wins.

The main game mechanics is deckbuilding. Players start with their basic decks and are able to gain new cards from the central display. During his turn a player decides which cards to play for their effect, and which to leave for bidding phase. The highes bidder is allowed to take first pick from the display of new cards.

Players main objective is to accumulate votes, and when all 21 votes are distributed among players the one with majority wins. In four-player variant two teams compete and one of them wins.

Players also fight for endorsement from priesthood, magnates and nobility to gain special cards providing them with once-per-turn effects.

Become a glider pilot - and start your journey into the aviation world! Gliders Racing is the world's first board game about gliding for both pilots and those who dream to be a pilot.

Practice the racing tactics, understand the meteorology and atmospheric phenomena which drive your flight and win the Big Race.
Enjoy realistic maps and cockpit views.
Have great fun while improving your flying skills.
Invite other players and compete to be the best glider pilot!

How does actual gliding work?
Do you know how does all this actually work? A glider (sometimes also called a sailplane) is an aircraft, that while flying is continuously losing altitude, up until it is so low, that the glider must land. The glider will commence its flight by being launched into the air either by an powered towing aeroplane or a stationary, ground-based winch. In such a way, a glider acquires its starting altitude, and may begin to lose it, thus beginning the flight. The glider can regain the altitude it lost by using atmospheric thermal updrafts. By utilizing such atmospheric phenomena, the glider can not only remain airborne for hours, but also cover large distances – gaining altitude in updrafts and losing it while traveling to another updraft. This is why it is crucial for the glider pilot to spend as much time as possible in updrafts, and as little time as possible in areas where are no updrafts. Such areas are called downdrafts.

Basic Rules.
Up to 4 players can play the game. Each player begins the game at the home airfield (START point), at the altitude of 1,500 meters. The goal of the game if to get to the FINISH point, visiting in succession all of the checkpoints, designated 1, 2 3, 4. The route between the points is the player’s choice. You can take the most direct, straight-line route, but other, less direct routes may be easier and more efficient. Remember: you need to visit all points in succession: START, 1, 2, 3, 4, and finally FINISH.

Flying throw the map you will loose the altitude which you can rebuild (under the cumulus cloud, ridge called on map sail or wave). Rebuild altitude takes a time, so in our game next player is moving at that time.

During all flight you have to keep over minimum your altitude and avoid out of landing which means you have start your journey again.

The winner is the one which will be first on finish.

-description from designer

The HABITATS XL expansion adds 16 tiles (and 3 game turns per player) :

- 8 animal tiles that need a larger habitat than any of the base game animals, and bring you more victory points if you fulfill their requirements.

- 4 camp site tiles, for your adventurous park visitors to spend the night. You score the victory points if the camp has all the indicated park connections satisfied. The better base it is to walk to different areas of your park, the more points it is worth.

- 4 flower tiles.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game (Second Edition) – The Blackwater
description:
The Blackwater is the fifth Chapter Pack in the King's Landing cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game.

The King’s Landing cycle takes a new approach to the Game of Thrones LCG. Rather than following the plot of A Song of Ice and Fire, this cycle is centered around the capital of the seven kingdoms, allowing you to explore the world of Westeros more freely than ever before. Every Chapter Pack in this cycle features three copies each of twenty unique cards to strengthen your decks and help you embrace the deadly intrigues of A Song of Ice and Fire with the continued cultivation of the shadow keyword, first reintroduced in the Dance of Shadows cycle.

Your journey to the capital of the seven kingdoms draws you beyond the city walls to explore the surrounding waterways that acted at the setting of the greatest battle in the War of the Five Kings. This battle was the making of many warriors, and you may elevate your own knights to the rank of Kingsguard if you deem them worthy. As shadows continue to creep in from the North, the Reach, and the streets of King's Landing itself, you may find that need more protection than ever if your House is to earn victory and claim the Iron Throne!

-description from the publisher

description:
PitchEasy is a game where you and your friends make easy creative pitches for genuine ideas. Using 30 real physical products and 90 real key phrases, there are over 80 million ideas that you could discover.

Each round starts with a FundIt card that sets out the pitching criteria for everyone. This sets out (1) how long each pitch should be, (2) the order of the cards that needs to be introduced, (3) if you are pitching on your own or as a team, and (4) the game mode - Study mode means you're allowed to look at your cards and Blind mode means that you aren't allowed looking at your cards until you start pitching.

Each player is dealt 3 Key Phrase cards and 1 Physical Product card. You then combine these cards according to the FundIt card's pitching criteria to create a new business idea.

After everyone pitches, each player votes for their favourite pitch/product idea by giving their Physical Product card to that player. The player with the most Physical Product cards ends the round and takes the FundIt card.

PitchEasy has 3 available versions:

    Filtered (SFW) - May contain adult humour.
    Unfiltered (NSFW) - Contains sexual innuendos and adult humour. 
    Filthy (Definitely NSFW) - Contains a lot  of sexual innuendos, adult humour and political incorrectness.


Find your 1 in a million idea today.

Castles of Dawnstone is a deck building and worker placement game for 1 or more players where you are trying to destroy your opponents village. Everyone starts with the same deck of 10 cards, but through individual village development and hiring mercenaries from the general pool you hopefully muster a better army than your opponent. In a turn, the active player plays all 5 cards in his hand and gains their benefits. Some cards may produce income to purchase mercenary cards or pay them off to retire so your opponent can't hire them. Other cards will allow you to attack your opponent outright. Some may increase the attack strength of your attacking cards. Others will allow you to build better buildings in your village, or worship at the alter of your Gods to give you the upper hand. There are lots of combinations for you to discover. Once you have played all of your cards, move any idle workers in your village to the designated area and leave the ones still working where they are. Then discard the cards you played and draw a new hand of 5 cards. Now your opponent takes their turn. That's it. Super simple rules with a complex strategy and high replay ability.

—description from the designer

House Ikaru is a global corporate behemoth. Its influence can be felt nearly everywhere in the material world and Aetherium alike. House Ikaru pioneered the AI programming that pushed deep into the Aetherium's wildest frontiers. Built on feudal principles of dynasty and conquest, the House is immortal. And now, few could possibly realize the extent of its true ambition.

Auxiliary Boxes contain 1 Avatar, 1 Function, and 1 Subroutine and are designed to supplement your favorite Collective with new and extraordinary strategic options. Each Box Set includes both PAD and Data cards for all programs.

This set contains 5 models including: Takona (Avatar), Crawler Bot (Function), Shinigami (Subroutine 3), 3 Data Cards, 3 PAD Cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Alchemy Struggle (アルケミストラグル)
description:
アルケミストラグル (Alchemy Struggle) is a Japanese card game, similar to Goita. It can be played as a 2 or 3 player game, as a 4 player game in partnership.

Players try to develop their potions as alchemists, and the first player who clears out their hand will win.

To win the game, it is important to determine which color of potion is rare. Because each time you create a potion unique in the world, you can take your turn again. So the players will aim to make potions which require more complex composition.

Further more you should keep in mind that you have a fellow player. By playing cards from your hand onto your partners potions, you will get closer to winning.

However, be careful, your fellow may secretly keep 'philosopher’s stone' card, a wild card in their hand. The philosopher's stone acts like a joker and can complete any potion. Knowing this is in play adds a thrill to the game.

The game was originally published in 2013 by Chicken Dice Games (チキンダイスゲームズ) and revised and re-issued by Southern Cross Games (サザンクロスゲームズ) in 2016.

boardgame: Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Queenie 15: New Quest Chambers
description:
This expansion adds three new Quest chambers: Riddle, Ascension and Guardian chambers

				
				
					The Riddle chamber (Quest)Players must solve the riddle of the four statues! When this chamber is revealed, the four statues are placed face down in the four spaces in the chamber. When players roll a set of symbols, they reveal the tile. Tiles must be revealed in the order indicated on the wall (blue, red, yellow, green). If a tile is revealed out of order, all statues are turned back over and players must start again.

				
				
					The Ascension chamber (Quest)In order to complete this task, players have to climb the steps in the chamber all the way to the top.

Players must climb from the bottom of the stairs to the top in sequence. When one player at the base of the steps or on a step rolls the required symbols for the next step, all players at the same level as that player in the chamber may move up to that step. This continues until the players reach the top of the steps, completing the Quest.

				
				
					The Guardian chamber (Quest)You are not the only ones interested in the gems of the temple - the magical guardians are as well.

When you enter the Guardian Quest chamber, one guardian token per player must be placed in the center of the room. Players may pick up one of these guardians by rolling two Green symbols, picking up the token, and placing a die on top of it.

The player must then move to a chamber where a gem can be activated (regardless of whether the gem has already been activated or not), at which point the guardian is discarded and your die recovered. When all of the tokens have been delivered, the quest is complete.

boardgame: Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Queenie 16: New Traps
description:
Escape: New Traps - Quicksand, Beast and Lava chambers

				
				
					The Quicksand chamber (Trap)When this trap is revealed, the player's adventurer is placed in the middle of the quicksand. They now only have a limited number of rolls to try and free themselves from impending doom. They can roll a maximum of 4 times and try to collect 4 torches to escape the quicksand. If they fail, they are stuck and cannot roll their dice again, until other players enter the chamber and roll the 4 torches to help them escape.

				
				
					The Beast chamber (Trap)You screech to a halt as you enter the room, trying to will your heart to beat more quietly, for in the corner, peacefully sleeping, lies a terrible beast.

To Escape the Beast Chamber, you will need to do something unique to the game—move slowly. You must roll only one die at a time, progressing your adventurer to the next symbol on the path as you roll it. If you roll a Black Mask symbol, set the die aside and use another one. If all your dice ever show Black Mask's, discard one die back to the game box and exit the chamber. Otherwise, when you roll the final symbol, you may exit the chamber and continue back on your frantic journey.

				
				
					The Lava chamber (Trap)The Lava Chamber presents a double challenge! When a player discovers the lava chamber, their adventurer is immediately placed in the pillar in the middle of the crater of lava. In order to Escape, they will have to roll 4 keys, with other players being able to help by contributing gold masks only. In addition, a gate token is placed across each entrance of the starting chamber, which must be removed by rolling the indicated symbols before players can use that entrance again.

Magic 2015 was released on July 18, 2014. Magic 2015 made the second major change to the card frame in Magic's history (the first being in Eighth Edition). Changes include a slight font change (Starting with Magic 2015, an in-house font known as Beleren will be used rather than the Matrix Bold font), the addition of a holofoil stamp in the bottom center of all rare and mythic rare cards, a slightly narrower black border, and a redesign of the collector's info at the bottom of each card. The new border will make it easier for machines to read the cards, helping to prevent mispackaging. Advertising for the set featured the planeswalker Garruk Wildspeaker, with a tagline of "Hunt Bigger Game."

Magic 2015 includes the mechanic "Convoke", which originally appeared in Ravnica: City of Guilds. This mechanic allows a player to use their creatures to help cast spells with Convoke. It also includes 15 cards designed by notable non-employee Magic fans, such as Richard Garriott, George Fan, and Notch, some of which also appear in Duels of the Planeswalkers 2015.

Core Set 2019, also known as Core 2019, is the 18th Magic core set. It is the first core set to be reintroduced under the Three-and-One Model and was released on July 13, 2018.
Core Set 2019 contains 280 cards (20 basic land, 111 common, 80 uncommon, 53 rare, and 16 mythic rare) and includes randomly inserted premium versions of all cards. 25 additional cards are found in the set's planeswalker decks. These are numbered #281/280 to #305/280. A unique Buy-a-Box promotional card numbered #306/280 is also considered to be part of the set. In addition, 8 common and uncommon cards from the Ixalan, Rivals of Ixalan and Dominaria sets (numbered #307/280 to #314/280) have been printed in the Welcome Decks with the Core Set 2019 expansion symbol. The common taplands sometimes replace a basic land in the land slot of booster packs. 181 cards are new, the rest is reprinted.

boardgame: A Game of Swords and Shields: PNP Edition
description:
Stanley Schuster's "A Game of Swords and Shields" is a turn based competitive deck building card battle game for 2 players from age 12. It lasts about 30 to 60 minutes and has a high replayability.
It is less about luck and chance as about strategy and decisions. Its game mechanic and the fact, that you can apply card effects on almost any faced up card, will give you a very unique experience.
Attack your opponent and defend yourself while you are building up an army of swords and shields until one of you loses all their hope or health points.

—description from the designer

Magic 2014 was released on July 19, 2013. The tagline for the set is "Ignite your Spark." As Bolas was the mascot of M13, Chandra was the mascot of M14. The returning mechanic of Magic 2014 is Slivers, a series of creatures of which each grants an ability to each Sliver.

Magic 2014 marked a change to the Legend rule. It made the "Indestructible" effect a keyword, and changed the phrasing for unblockable creatures to "can't be blocked." Slivers in Magic 2014 also worked subtly differently from Slivers in earlier Magic; they now only affected Slivers owned by the same controller, rather than all Slivers in the game. Slivers also received an art redesign that de-emphasized their original beak-headed, one-clawed, one-tailed insect-like appearance, and instead became monstrous humanoids whose appearance varied heavily by card, but had "normal" features such as faces and eyes. This redesign proved controversial; one reviewer noted "slivers are one of the most iconic designs in all of Magic: The Gathering. To essentially muddle them down into just another humanoid monster thing is really disappointing." Wizards of the Coast acknowledged the negative feedback, noting that some players disliked the new art style, and included a card in Magic 2015 that used the original Sliver appearance in Sliver Hive

Magic Origins was released on July 17, 2015. Magic Origins told the origin stories for 5 planeswalkers who are featured in sets after Origins. It featured a cycle of double-faced cards (originally used in Innistrad) that have a legendary creature on one side representing the character before their transformation, and a planeswalker on the reverse face that represents them after gaining their new power.

The set introduced the mechanics of renown, spell mastery, and menace. It also features cards with the prowess mechanic, which was introduced in Khans of Tarkir block, and the scry mechanic, which was introduced in Mirrodin block.

In February 2015, Wizards of the Coast said that it would be an introductory product "like a core set", and it was published in the time period that a core set would have taken up during the pattern established by Magic 2010. After Origins, Magic switched to a new schedule where each year contained 2 blocks, and each block contained 2 sets. With the new schedule, there are no announced plans for a major "standalone" set like Origins that is not part of a block.

Show off your inner zen as a disciple of the Monkey King Sun Wukong!

King Monkey has decreed that his tribe of monkeys must create a kingdom worthy of his status as Great Sage, Equal of Heaven on Mount Huaguo (Fruit and Flowers Mountain). To do so his disciples must tame the forest by collecting and placing landscape tiles - Each player creating a lovely wild garden full of ponds, paths and ornamental features that show their understanding of Monkey King's teachings and the Six Principles of Zen.

Zen Monkeys is a relaxing tile-laying, resource gathering, set collection worker-placement game for everyone; from younger players and new gamers, to families and more experience players. The game looks deceptively simple but has a lot of choice and ways to score under the lid as with only 18 quick rounds in the game, its a race to grab what you need, get building and impress Sun Wukong!

Players compete to balance exploitation and conservation in an effort to save endangered species from the brink of extinction. To do this, players must accumulate money through the collection and selling of resources in order to then buy and conserve land. To collect resources, players must exploit the land, and potentially push species to extinction. Players take the role of advisers to the Minister for the Environment - choosing when to approve requests for resource projects (collecting resources for doing so) and choosing which companies to give the approved contracts to (owned by other players, or themselves - who also collect resources). The role of the Minister is to conserve species, therefore the adviser that enables the best conservation of species wins the game! The game ends when either 15 species are saved, or 6 are made extinct.

The setup for the game is always randomized: The lands are arranged at random, in addition to the location of the endangered species; The resource cost to improve the efficiency of exploited lands is randomized; and the resource cost to move species, create habitat bridges and recycle waste is also randomized.

The board consists of 4 separate land types (Plains, Oceans, Mountains, Forests) with 20 endangered species. Players compete to collect 6 different resource types: Food (from Plains), Oil (from Oceans), Metal (from Mountains), Wood (from Forests), Electricity (from Electricity plants - built anywhere) and People (from Cities - built anywhere). They do this by buying and exploiting the land: spreading out from a starting land to adjacent land tiles. The cost of land grows as more of it is exploited. Players must also pay the cost of Waste that is generated by exploiting lands and extracting resources.

A turn has two phases. Phase 1 involves: Approving resource projects (3 available each turn, drawn from a deck), Buying land, Buying/Selling resources, Upgrading lands and/or performing Acts of Conservation (building habitat bridges, relocating species, recycling waste). Phase 2 involves: Recycling waste at Recycling plants (built anywhere) and Paying Waste dues (mandatory).

—description from the designer

boardgame: Thunder Upon the Water: The Battle of Albemarle Sound, May 5, 1864
description:
Thunder Upon the Water details the battle of Albemarle Sound fought on May 5th, 1864. Via the sound’s numerous ports Confederate blockade runners brought badly needed supplies for the rebel nation. In April a Confederate army supported by the new ironclad CSS Albemarle, captured the Union held port of Plymouth. Encouraged by this success, Confederate General Robert Hoke planned an offensive to recapture the city of Bern, held by Union forces for nearly two years.

Captain of the CSS Albemarle, James W. Cooke, steamed out to battle in afternoon of May 5th supported by two gunboats, the recently captured Union gunboat CSS Bombshell and the CSS Cotton Plant. The Confederate “Mosquito Fleet” ran into a Union squadron of eight gunboats, five of them new “double ender” side wheel steamers designed as rams to attack Confederate ironclads. The resulting battle was a classic of strength vs. numbers. The Union ships held off the Confederates, but both sides were severely bloodied. Can you do as well or better than your historical counterpart?

Each copy of Thunder Upon the Water is composed of the following:

•    One 11 by 17 inch hex map sheet.
•    One sheet of 142 single-sided, un-mounted counters
•    One Players’ Aid Sheet
•    One set of rules

Players use a standard deck of playing cards to activate their units and resolve some game events. Players also use six and ten sided dice (one of each). Each turn represents 15 minutes of time. Each hex is a little more than 200 yards across. Each unit represents 1 warship. The game is designed by Paul Rohrbaugh and features graphics by Bruce Yearian.

A supplemental card set for the game is also available. It replaces the standard deck of playing cards called for in the rules, feature graphics that compliment the game’s, and has rules reminders on pertinent cards.

-description from designer

Kick On is a collectible card game in which players will face off taking on the role of soccer team trainers, organizing their teams, strategizing plays and formations and converting goals to become the best trainer in the championship.

—description from the publisher

PREPARING THE GAME

Each player will be able to customize a deck of 45 gameplay cards (Play, Strategy, Foul and Energy cards) and 10 Player cards, in five categories of strength and rarity (Amateur, Semi-professional, Professional and Legendary, the latter admitted only once per deck) and use these strategically in combination with the rolling two six-sided dice to determine certain aspects of the different gameplay interaction’s outcomes.

The customization of the deck takes place before playing, and different configurations can bring about setup bonuses (for instance, starting with more Energy Cards if one of your Players has the ‘Captain’ ability).

GAMEPLAY

The playing area is divided into a series of Zones: the play zones (Defense and Attack), the Lines of Formation, the Bench, the Exhausted Cards, and the Showers.
Player cards have certain stats and special abilities. There are different Lines of Formation on the pitch in which they can be situated (Goal, Defense, Middle-field, Attack) and begin play already on either the pitch’s play zones or the Bench (not the player’s hand). Cards engaged in the current action move into the Defense of the Attack Zones.
All Play, Strategy and Foul Cards have an Energy cost to be paid with the use of Energy Cards, which are then Exhausted until the following turn. All resolved Play, Strategy and Foul Cards, as well as expelled Player Cards, are all sent to the Showers. Players from the Bench can be brought into play through the “Exchange” Strategy Card or if a player’s Player is Injured by one of the other player’s Foul Cards.

Gameplay is carried out in turns and each turn consists of five phases: 1) Energy, 2) Tactical Advise, 3) Attack or 4) Defense and 5) Taking a Shot.

During these phases the players may sequentially:

     either spend or regroup Energy or Player cards;
     use their Strategy Cards;
     select some Players and attack; or 
     use Strategy and Foul Cards to defend; and
     Take a Shot for the goal, comparing Player defense and attack stats modified through dice roll to determine the success of the shot- if successful, they score a goal.


- Penalties can happen on Phase 4) by annulling a Foul card with the Strategy Card “Penalty” or as a tie-breaker at the end of the game.

As soon as one of the player’s decks runs out the game is over and the player whose team converted the most goals is the winner.

—user summary

In Sovereignty, you must expand your empire economically and militarily until you achieve world domination. Expand your territory by building forts in uncontested lands or by capturing enemy forts. Develop your economy by exploiting natural resources and trading with minor nation ports. Invest in technology for long-term strategic advantages. Recruit an army and build a fleet to protect your empire’s interests and conquer what is rightfully yours!

Begin the game by building a map out of the 20 unique land and 24 sea tiles which snap together for fast, stable assembly. Tiles feature 7 rare resources, which each provide a special bonus that will influence your strategy. This hex-based map will become the battlefield where you must position your armies tactically to threaten enemy forts while still defending your own. Battles involve revealing your army cards against your opponent’s cards, and are resolved based entirely on the regiment composition and technological advantages – there are no dice or random draws, just imperfect information. Naval fleets provide strategic support to your armies, allowing you to quickly transport troops or even blockade your enemy’s capital to try to starve their economy. Players are eliminated once their capital fort is conquered.

While Sovereignty is primarily a competitive strategy game, it also features a more story-driven campaign mode for solo/cooperative play. Become Napoleon in his campaign to conquer Europe (and with enough skill, the world). Engage in historic battles against an automated opponent, driven by behavior cards that react to your decisions. Each province you capture on the world map provides benefits for future battles, so you gain strength between each game you play.

Sovereignty was designed to be relative quick to learn, but difficult to master. The modular map, rare resources, and technology systems all result in a large diversity of strategies, so every game is unique. The artwork and thematic elements are all designed to provide an immersive experience, so you really can feel like you are building and growing an empire.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dobbers: Quest for the Key – 6 Player Expansion!
description: Expands Dobbers: Quest for the Key with up to two more players

Smugglers is a humorous retro-style board game set in the late 1980's, where players smuggle goods in old cars between Norway and Sweden. During the game players choose missions with low, medium or high risk/reward, and move on a network of roads using dice. Players can buy helpful items such as a punch card for access to a safer and shorter route, nitro for additional speed, and a trailer for moving more cargo pr. run. Speed is key to outrun the police, and to reclaim stolen cargo/money from hijackers. There's also items for bribing both police and customs personnel, a possibility otherwise determined by dice. The more beaten up a car is the more it increases the chance of getting noticed at the border, so outer damage should be fixed when possible at a repair shop. Damage to the vehicle or cargo, finding extra money, and many other good or bad situations are determined by dice, as well as cards with thematic and often comical descriptions. After 4 rounds consisting of 30 turns each, the smuggler with the largest fortune on a secret bank account is the winner!

The game's retro-style isn't only due to its late 80's setting, but also because both game-play and components (one-colored cards and tokens) are in line with a release from a simpler era in board games. Such homages are often seen in electronic games, so instead - here's one in board games.

—description from the designer

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

From the rear of the box:
Founded on principles of excellence, equality and opportunity for all, the University of York opened in 1963 with just 230 students. In less than 50 years it has become one of the world's leading universities, carving out a reputation as an academic powerhouse where a clear focus on excellence has secured national and international recognition alongside longer established institutions.
A member of the elite Russell Group of universities, it is a dynamic, research-intensive university committed to the development of life-saving discoveries and new technologies to tackle some of the most pressing global challenges. There are now over 30 academic departments and research centres and the student body has expanded to nearly 16,000.
Located within walking distance of York city centre, our safe and attractive Heslington campus is home to our nine colleges and most of our departments. We also have several departments located in the city centre at the historic King's Manor. In recent years, our £750m campus expansion has seen the opening of seven new buildings, increasing our capacity for student numbers and providing more world-class facilities for the 21st Century. Sustainability plays a key role in this ambitious development.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

From the rear of the box:
The University of Leicester was founded in 1921 as a living memorial to World War I. The aim of the men and women who built the institution was that sacrifices made in in war should be commemorated through education and research that would change the world for the better. Our values reflect our motto Ut Vitum Habeant - 'so that they may have life', and today our University is a lively energetic and global community, in which dedicated and talented people are working towards, and helping people shape, the future.  We are committed to research that saves, improves and enriches lives, and to being a University that is accessible to talented students from all backgrounds.
Whether you are a current Leicester student, graduated years ago or are a member of staff, we hope you enjoy University of Leicester MONOPOLY and it captures the spirit of your Leicester experience.

Freakin' Fractals is a 2-4 player tabletop game where players race to be the first to fill in their side of the pyramid with all the same colored fractals!  The pyramid can unexpectedly be turned.  Or pieces can be stolen.  Or cards can be taken.  Frustrating fun!
Each player gets one TAKE OR STEAL THE LARGE FRACTAL card.  Throw the rest of the cards onto the table and mix them up.  After sufficient randomizing has occurred, each player should draw four cards.  All remaining cards go into the draw pile.
Youngest player starts the game play, of course. Try to fill in your side with the same colored fractals, before the pyramid is turned.  When the pyramid in turned, whatever color is facing you is your new side.  After you play one card from your hand, place that card in the discard pile and draw a new card; you should always have FIVE cards.  The game continues clockwise.  If the draw pile runs out, shuffle the discard pile and it becomes the draw pile.
First player to fill in a side of the fractal with all the same color, and then place the LARGE fractal in his or her side, and exclaim "FREAKIN’ FRACTALS" wins!  The large 'mega' fractal must be placed after the other three same colors on that side.  For a longer game, decide the first player who wins three games is the ultimate champion.

—description from the designer

Find out what our future President will do when their golf courses are threatened by a cannibal incursion, the milk delivery is hijacked on the way to the Bullet Farm, and oh man oh man, your daughter is so, so hot. The hottest. Because what's more fun than a dismayingly dark future in which everything we love is threatened by uncertainty? It’s a Dear Leader hack set in a (what was imagined at the time to be unthinkable) near future of the U.S.A.

—description from the designer

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

From the rear of the box:
Accra, which elegantly stretches along the Atlantic coast of Ghana, is the country’s capital and largest city, with an estimated urbanpopulation of 2.3 million.
Originally built around a port, it served the capital of the British Gold Coast between 1877 and 1957.  Accra has since transitioned into a modern, growing metropolis, the city reflects a history ranging from the 19th century architecture to modern skyscrapers and apartment blocks.
Accra is the home to the Kotoka International Airport and is your gateway to original Ghanaian hospitality, peace and joie de vivre! Accra is also home to many restaurants and “chop bars”, where you can sample authentic, divine-tasting Ghanaian and continental dishes.  The city is vibrant and serves as Ghana’s economic, administrative, cultural and entertainment hub.  The world famous Azonto dance, among several other dance genres, originated from Accra.  Accra is also home to some beautiful sandy beaches.  Tourism is thriving in Accra and the country as a whole.
In 2010, the Globalization and World Cities Research Network designated Accra a Gamma-minus-level world city, indicating a growing level of international influence and connectedness.  Its entry into the internationally acclaimed MONOPOLY family underscores its unique status as an emerging tourism and business city.
The City of Accra Edition provides an exceptional city tour.  It captures the rich diversity of the city’s exciting offerings and highlights the extent to which it has evolved over the past decade.  You can now play this classic game with Accra’s street names and some of its most recognised landmarks.  Savour a piece of the true magic that the City of Accra exudes, and experience its energy and pace.  At the same time you will learn about Ghana from an architectural and historical point of view.
Find your favourite Accra hotspot, roll the dice and build your property empire from Nima to Ridge via Airport City in this special edition of this most famous board game.  In 2013, the New York Times, named Accra as one of its top Travel destinations and listed it 4th on their “46 places to go in 2013”.  The prestigious paper described the city as “a buzzing metropolis ready for business and pleasure”.
“Charley, welcome aboard!”

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

From the rear of the box:
The FIFA World Cup is the ultimate prize in the world's most popular sport, won only by those with belief, skill and good fortune on their side.
You'll need all of those qualities as well as the roll of the dice, to win MONOPOLY: 2018 FIFA Worfld Cup edition!/p>
You'll find the world's heavyweights and many others in the game, as the cream of world football find their home together in one place, and for the first time, this prestigious event will be hosted in Russia.
Join Zabivalia to see who will be crowned champion of MONOPOLY, the 2018 FIFA World Cup edition.

Promo Card for Call to Adventure originally available at Origins 2019.

At end of game, if you have Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma, gain 9 Triumph.

boardgame: So You Think You Know Cats
description:
So You Think You Know Cats is an educational family game. There are cards, divided into 3 categories, with a total of 900 questions and answers. Players roll dice to see which category (either Breed, General, or Rare/Exotic) question they must answer and how many points the question is worth. The player may keep going until an incorrect answer is given then play passes to the next player going clockwise. The first player to 250 points wins the first place ribbon. To make the game more challenging, the point total may be increased or the questions may be asked without giving the multiple choice answers when they are present.

When Leaf Village comes under attack while most of their ninja
are out on various missions, it falls to a small handful of ninja to protect the village! Together, the members of Team 7 and a
couple of their close friends will attempt to turn back the
invaders. They are the last line of defense, and they must not

TEAM 7’s heores as they fight back an invading force of
enemies that fans of Naruto Shippuden will be quick to
recognize. Play as your favorite Naruto character and face off against the series classic villains. Build combos and focus your chakra to unleash massive attacks!

The Animated Series Faction Pack is a pre-built fleet of four ships for Federation, Klingons and Romulans, based on Star Trek - The Animated Series: the U.S.S. Enterprise, two Klingon D7 battlecruisers, and a Romulan D7 battlecruiser. Included are ships, tokens, dials, bases and 29 cards. Everything needed to fly the ships straight out of the box.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Borg Faction Pack: Resistance is Futile

The Borg Faction Pack: Resistance is Futile is a pre-built fleet of four ships for the Borg faction: a Borg sphere, a Borg scout cube, an assimilated Galaxy class ship, and an assimilated Intrepid class ship. Included are ships, tokens, dials, bases and 28 cards. Everything needed to fly the ships straight out of the box.

The Munchkin Mat of Mayhem is a Level tracker with a twist: every space (except lowly Level 1) has a game rule! Choose your path carefully to get the right bonus at the right time.

This 24-inch square neoprene playmat will look fantastic in the middle of your game table, with your favorite miniature (or Munchkin pawn) showing your hero's rapid progress to Level 10... and victory!

Based on the manga and anime series, One Punch Man.

Jump into the world of One Punch Man and play as the greatest heroes on Earth!

Make combos to beat the monsters that have invaded our planet. But be careful, don't wait too long, otherwise Saitama will come to settle their score with a single punch before you have had time to do so!

Play Hero cards from your hand to make combos and deal damage to unveiled monsters. Apply the effects of cards to strengthen your line of combo or on the contrary, undo that of your opponents. But do not waste time, because Saitama approaches the enemy and will eliminate him at once if he arrives at him before you have finished!

3 Creatures

"Viking Fury" is a strategic level solitare wargame covering the initial Viking expansion into western Europe in the late 8th-early 9th century. The player controls  the Viking side and attempts to acquire treasure for his home location, while potentially planting new settlements across the sea. Each game turn represents a variable amount of actual time. The object of the game is to bring as much treasure back home to Scandinavia (and establish settlements over the sea) within a set number of expeditions.

Components
Viking Fury comes with eight pages of rules, a game map, and a game counter set. You will also need one six-sided die, and a standard deck of playing cards (52 cards with no jokers) to play.

—description from the publisher

description:
Battle over Europe is a two-player game (with a solitaire option) strategy card game of World War II air combat, based upon single plane-vs.-plane dogfights occurring in various theaters in the early years of the conflict, specifically, up through the end of 1941. Gamers fly historical Allied and Axis warplanes in fast and furious dogfights, fighting for control of the sky on a Dogfight Display, using aircraft counters that are maneuvered into position by playing cards.

The key to winning is having positional Advantage over your opponent during play. Each plane is authentically rated in a two-player version with six short campaign game scenarios. The extended Example of Play will have you flying in no time!

—description from the publisher

North Cape is a strategy solitaire or two-player wargame simulating the Battle of North Cape in 1943 between German and British naval forces.

North Cape uses the rules of Advanced Salvo! European Theater, as modified for this particular engagement.

Components
North Cape comes with a set of counters, a Sea Display and Game Tables and five pages of rules. You will also need three six-sided dice, and paper and pencil.  It is recommended to mount the North Cape counters.

—description from the publisher

Science Ninja Cowboy is an action-packed card game where you and your friends face off in a no-holds-barred battle to the death!

Each player starts with five cards in their hand, and five cards face down in front of them as lives. You take turns drawing and playing cards, and battle through each round until the only player left with lives wins the game.

You can attack each other’s lives with a plethora of weapons, protect your own with hidden counters, and place each other in random life-threatening situations until only one of you is left standing. 
 Tension builds each round as alliances form, revenge is taken, and players move one step closer to their inevitable victory or demise!

—description from the designer

You drop into the red zone. You're immediately surrounded by enemies. Are they human or something else? It doesn't matter. You've brought along enough firepower to secure this area... once and for all.

Take control of a faction of humans battling in an apocalyptic landscape — one overrun with zombies. Can you fend off your opponent's forces while avoiding the roaming hordes of undead? You better hope so, or you'll be just another casualty of Area Z.

If you can’t get enough of area control games with clever action selection, you won’t want to miss this little gem. Playing 2-3 players, you’re not only vying for control of areas of the map against your foes, but you are also fending off masses of zombies who will pick you off if left unchecked. Each card has two sides, and every card will be used twice per round (one on each side) before moving to the zombie phase - and the order in which the cards get used will determine how the zombies react and interact on the map.

—description from the publisher


This 9-card game published by Button Shy pits you and one or two other players in an area control game against each other... and zombies. Lay out the three map cards in any formation, choose one of the three colors and begin play!

Play consists of three rounds with each including a Survivor Phase, Zombie Phase, and Scoring Phase. Players select an action card for their team, then the zombies act according to the back side of the selected cards, and then area control points are divvied. Players also gain points for killing zombies.


Released in the April 2019 Board Game of the Month Club $10+ package.

The popular card game in sheep's clothing!

Who will be the first to get rid of all the cards?

Not easy, if "Doppelböcke" or "Jammerhammel" repeatedly prevents playing the cards!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Das beliebte Kartenspiel im Schafspelz!

Nicht einfach, wenn Doppelböcke oder Jammerhammel immer wieder das Ablegen der Karten verhindern!

boardgame: Zheng He Goes to the West Coast
description:
Starting from a city state in Mesopotamia, you expand your empire to every corner of the Old World, and finally set sail to invade the Americas. The entire history of the human race is replayed within an hour.
You roll dice and fill the numbers in the blanks on your world map. Choose your dice cleverly, as the rest are used by the other players.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Rifles, Railroads and Rebellions: Fast Play Wargame for the American Civil War and Later 19th Century Conflicts
description:
Rifles, Railroads and Rebellions is based upon C’est la Guerre: Fast Play Wargame for the Age of Muskets. This system was designed to provide a fast yet satisfying game play, not necessarily a “realistic” simulation of battle recreation. Bases or ‘Units’ in the game do not represent a certain number of men or artillery pieces but are abstract representations of force.

Rifles, Railroads and Rebellions uses

- a simple command structure to manage battlefield movement without dealing with morale or having the players issue orders,

- a troop grading system so that better trained more effective troops can have an advantage over poorly trained less effective troops, and

- a method to account for casualties and losses without an overly complicated accounting system.

Rifles, Railroads and Rebellions is an abstraction. A Unit in the game does not necessarily equal a particular organizational unit, formation in the field, or a specific number of soldiers. Each base is a representation of force, and as such issues such as the size of one battalion, regiment, brigade, or squadron should be set aside. The player should concern himself with tactics such as troop placement, command access, gun placement and terrain features. The goal is to recreate the “feel” of the period, not necessarily every detail of it.

-description from publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
ピザオク (Pizaoku) Pizza Auction! is a bidding game with a theme of eating pizza.

In a round, players secretly choose a pizza card, place it face-down and declare which type of pizza they are baking (they may tell a lie). Then they simultaneously place their bid on a pizza card they want.

If only one player has placed on a pizza, that player takes it. If there is more than one player on a pizza, the auction continues until all but one player passes. If no one bids on a pizza, the player who baked that pizza takes it.

You are eliminated if you are forced to take total of 9 or more quarters of pizza. 
You win the game if you have accumulated 4 quarters of the same type without being eliminated.

プレイヤーはお腹をすかせたピザ好きの食いしん坊たちです。

このゲームは3つのステップを繰り返します。
1)ピザを焼く
2)ピザオークション
3)ピザを食べる

同じ種類のピザを、1ホール食べたプレイヤー、もしくはポテトを3個分食べたプレイヤーの勝利です。
publisher's summary

description:
Cultist/Occultist has you taking on the role of either trying to save the world from utter destruction, or trying to usher into it your dark god from beyond time and space. This is a social deduction game where there's more to do than just figure out who is on the other team, you also need to strategize to stop them!

Each turn of the game will have the players collectively trying to start or prevent a ritual from taking place. The leader of the ritual will determine which one to attempt This is done by contributing different components to the ritual that will either aid or hinder its attempted effect. Be careful though! Too many failed ritual attempts can have another bad effect.

Each ritual slightly alters the fabric of reality, and can change how the game itself plays. Some rituals will make future rituals easier to pass, while others will give certain players new abilities and things that they can do. Between this and different Great Old Ones, no two play-throughs will be the same.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Uncaged: Brazilian Jiu Jitsu Starter Deck
description:
Tap, Nap or Snap. The Choice is Yours.

After rising to dominance following the first UNCAGED tournament, Brazilian Jiu Jitsu master Rei has gone missing. With another tournament on the horizon, who will take his place representing the gentle art? Enter 3 of his pupils, all seeking to prove their mastery!

The Uncaged: Brazilian Jiu Jitsu Starter deck can be played stand alone out of the box, or combined with other UNCAGED decks.

—description from the publisher

Flow like water.

Three Kung Fu experts have arrived at the Uncaged tournament to represent their ancient martial art! Behold Eagle, Monkey and Mantis! A deadly dance of fists cementing Kung Fu's rightful place on the world stage!

The Uncaged: Kung Fu Starter deck can be played stand along out of the box, or combined with other Uncaged decks.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Uncaged: Training Gym. Deck Building Essentials
description:
It's time to step up your game.

Get back in the gym and hone your fighter's skills with this booster set of punches, kicks, submissions and takedowns. Be ready to inflict some serious damage standing or on the ground. But remember, the only fighting style that matters is yours!

This is not a standalone product. Use your Training Gym to customize or build your own Uncaged: World Fighters deck.

—description from the publisher

Two player game played with different colored pegs on 4x4 grid of holes.

"Each player uses different color pegs. The object of the game is to get your pegs of the same color in a square in all four corners, four in a row up and down, across, or diagonally at the same time try to block your opponent from scoring a Super TTT  first... Just like ordinary Tic Tac Toe but a lot more fun."

From the simplicity of the game it would seem to mean a square of pegs in a 2x2 grid location. An interesting variation might include any geometric square.

2-player card game about Ice Hockey. No knowledga of Ice Hockey needed!
First player to have 3 goals wins.
Players play cards from their deck into a 3x2 game area for each player. Cards are compared in each 3 column. Winner in each column has a greater card values in that column. Then each winning columns are added together and highest value wins the situation (turn).

It´s possible to win with a lower value cards a player that plays higher value cards. That´s when cards are played wisely.

IH introduces an interesting new game mechanic: Deduction Deck Mechanic.

—description from the designer

DDM is a card mechanic where two values are compared. Winning value (higher) and losing value (lower). Then from results deck is drawn as many cards as is the winning value. The losing party (player) then deducts (eliminates) the most bad cards from that set: Goals and worst Penalties. 
After that the winning player chooses from the remaining cards the outcome of the situation: Goal, Penalties, Powerplay etc.

The players compete in 10 sports. The results for each sport are determined in different ways by rolling 1 or 2 dice and are marked by meeples on the game board. For each sport points are awarded which are written on a piece of paper. The player with the most points wins the game.

—user summary

Uptown is a 3D-stacking strategy game where players take turns placing a tetracube of any colour on the game board trying to dominate at least four columns.

COMPONENTS

- A checkered game board with 35 squares arranged in seven columns.

- 8 red and 8 grey tetracubes.

A tetracube is a geometric form that consists of four cubes joined.

HOW TO PLAY
 Each player has an allocated colour (red or grey). Red player begins. Take turns placing a tetracube of any colour on the game board according to the following constraints:

1. At least one of its cubes must rest directly on the board, within a square.

2. No cube may rest outside the game board boundaries.

3. No cube may “hang in the air” without support from below.

The game ends when the player in turn has no legal moves.

WINNING THE GAME

The score is set column by column. A column is dominated by the colour that occupies at least three of the five squares when the game board is looked upon from above. The player dominating at least four columns wins the game.

-description from designer

boardgame: Robotech: Force of Arms – Grand Cannon Promo Expansion
description:
This is a 5-card promo expansion (including 1new "Ship" Card -ThevGrand Cannon. 1new Hero and Command Card for each Player

To use The Grand Cannon, replace the "Empty Space" Card from the main game and place The Grand Cannon Card in the center of the space sector during the Sey Up.

When the players move ships during the tactical phase of the game, the Grand canyon can be selected only as the second card to be moved. The Grand canyon can only switch places with a ship if it is orthodontically adjacent (Above, Below, Left, or Right)

Found in the back of Game Time Magazine handed out during Origins 2019.

Xero-G is a sci-fi themed strategy game designed to be played with Components: Looney Pyramid Games. It is a two-player game with zero luck and perfect information, played on a 6×6 grid.

Players take on the role of spaceship pilots from warring planets, each competing to the be first to invade their opponent's alien base. Pyramids represent vacant spaceships available for use by either player. Movement is orthogonal, increases with the size of the ship, and can be supplemented by boosts and special movement rules.

Collect cargo, load it onto your train, then send off the train to score points based on the market values of the cargo at the time. Lower the values of your opponent's cargo while keeping what is on your own train high, upgrade your locomotive to pull longer trains, steal and sabotage, and choose a route to send your train along to score bonus points.

Trains & Cargo is a strategic, casual board game for 2-4 or 6 players, individually or in teams. There are two types of locomotives, eight types of cargo (coal, timber, corn, cars, steel, food, cattle and passengers) and three Route Boards that you can claim to score bonus points.

Each turn you may choose to either play cards from your hand, upgrade your locomotive, or to send off your train to score points for the cargo it holds. There are three types of cards and you may play up to one of each type on any given turn: Cargo Cards to collect cargo from a central bank, Market Cards to increase or decrease the values of specified cargo, and Effect Cards with various text-based abilities.

Things to consider when playing Trains & Cargo include what cards to combine for maximum effect, how long to wait until you send off your train, if you should wait for a specific route to become available or choose a different path, and more. The game is entirely free of play money which means that getting a head start early on does not mean a certain win - you are limited by the cards in your hand, not the money in your bank.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Canine Chaos Card Game: Celebrity Edition
description:
Join this a-list cast of canine celebrities for a fast and frantic Card-Swapping game of canine craziness.

In Canine Chaos, players compete to be the first to complete three matching sets of celebrity dogs in this matching, set collection game.

The first to complete 3 sets wins, but play continues until all players have completed their sets.

—description from the publisher

Name It is a Draw-a-card-&-write game which is based on an Iranian traditional party game called Esm-o-Famil (First name and family name).

In Name It, players try to name as many words in various categories as they can with the each letter card revealed.
There are also a deck of Event Cards, starting a cool challenge in every round of the game. The main part of the game is writing, but some parts are done orally.

In every round a card revealed which has a letter on it and then all players simultaneously try to write down a word with that letter in categories shown on a category card (which is varied from personal to entertainment). Each player scores for his/her right words and at the end the player with the most points wins the game.

—user summary

Enchanters: Odyssey is a game-altering expansion, that comes with:

     variable starting powers - become Dwarven Cultist or Gnoll Druid and use your talents to guide you to victory
     physical coop and solo mode - a new mode that lets you play with your * non-competitive friends or no friends at all
     6 new Kingdoms (with corresponding Overlords and Villages) - each with a new distinct mechanic to combine with previous decks
     All themed around the journey through mythological antiquity.


—description from the designer

The Railroads of Bornholm is a member of the 18xxx series of games. 1899+1 is set on the Danish island Bornholm. Here the railways came late (in 1900) so that explains the name of the game. You can call it 18Born if you don't like the pun.

Two, three, or four players represent investors in railroad companies, spending their initial capital to purchase shares in railway companies. The companies, each controlled by the largest shareholder, build track and run trains to earn revenue. These can be paid out as dividends to shareholders to retained to fund further track and trains. A key feature of 1899+1, as with all other games in the series, is that the interest of the President and the interest of the shareholders are not identical, so minority shareholders need to be careful with their investments. Being a President is not without its difficulties, either; bailing out an insolvent company is the sole responsibility of its President.

1899+1, while short by standards of other members of the series, is a fairly long game. Experienced players can expect to take up to three hours to complete a game.

Mamlejet Kohanim is a semi-collaborative collectible card game, set in the time of the first Kings of ancient Israel, in which each player assumes the role of Commander of a Division of the King's Army. The players are allied to each other, and the objective of the game is to be the first to demoralize the enemy army through different tactics: soldiers, objects, and other strategy cards. The combats are resolved by dice rolls.

—description from the publisher

With nightfall, the lotus sinks underwater to rest until the new day.  However, the pond does not rest and new creatures begin to emerge.

Thrive: Pond Life offers players new ways to play Thrive.  More colored pegs allow a 3rd and 4th player to the pond, variable power cards "bend" the normal rules of play, and variable setups keep your pond fresh.  The Pond Life also includes a brand new variant to discover a completely new way to play inside.

—description from the publisher

An exciting way to learn about producers and consumers!  Producers and Consumers is an educational game designed to teach the basic economic concepts of production, consumption, bartering and specialization to students in grades 1-3.

Each player draws a card depicting a profession and gets several resource tokens related to their job.  On the back of their card is their goal--the resources they must collect to win.  Players must then trade to try to collect all the resources they need.  The first player to collect all the necessary resources wins.

The game can be played at three levels.  At level one, resources are plentiful and all players should be able to complete their cards with simple one-to-one trades.  At level two, some resources are scarcer and some players have a smaller supply, so one-to-one trades may not be sufficient to complete cards.  At level three, there are not enough resources for all players to complete their cards, so competition for some resources may be fierce!

5 small plastic pieces which are tossed from the hand to play several different games. The boxed game includes a rules sheet and a small drawstring bag to hold everything.

The plastic pieces are shaped like "knucklebones" (astragalus) and are in 5 different bright colors.

There are 25 games or challenges in the rules. The games would involve things like tossing the 5 pieces from palm and catching them in palm. Or tossing them from palm and catching them on the back of the hand.

—user summary

Fly Guy's Buzz Words Game is an educational game allowing early readers to practice sight words.

The game comes in a "trash can" with a 3D Fly Guy character on top.  Players are dealt a hand of very small blue cards with common one-syllable words.  The same words appear on a deck of red cards.

Players take turns drawing a card from the red deck, reading the word aloud and then trying to make a match with a blue card they hold.  It they match, they yell "Swat!" and put the matching cards back in the can by dropping them through Fly Guy's mouth.

If the active player cannot make a match, other players have an opportunity to match the drawn red card in the same way.

The red deck also includes "Lose a Turn" and "Give 1 Card to Any Player" cards.  The latter allows the current player to give one of his blue cards to another player before taking his/her turn normally.

The first player to throw all of his or her cards into the garbage buzzes to victory.

A quick, engaging game of treasure hunting with a twist!

Welcome to Pirate Quest! Be the Pirate Ghost, create the treasure island by laying down cards randomly, and then hide the treasure. Then follow the actions of the other pirates as they try to find the ultimate treasure chest.

Stop them, confuse them, move them around the island, and gather their willpower in order to release your captured soul!

In Pirate Quest one player is the ghost of a long forgotten pirate captain who tries to stop the other pirates finding his ultimate treasure chest. The players will move around the randomized island trying to dig and figure out where the chest is. Every action they take fills the Will power of the ghost. If they cannot find the chest in time, the ghost will be released and everyone will lose. But if one of them finds the chest, he or she will be the winner!

description:

Change Up is a fast-paced, easy to learn game with three rounds. It's important to keep track of where the Point Cards end up. You'll place the five Point Cards face down on the table in order from 0 to 4. Then, everyone will draw six cards to make a hand letting them do things like move the face down Point Cards, mess with other players' hands, and get the chance to pick first to get that elusive 4-point Point Card. Record the score you get from the card at the end of each round. Whoever has the highest score at the end of three rounds is the winner.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dust 1947: PLA/Red Army Sarnai and Grom
description:
Captain “Red Lightning” Sarnai
Hero of the Sino-Soviet Union – 1946
Hero of the Soviet Union – 1944
Order of Lenin – 1944
Order of the Patriotic War – 1943
Order of the Red Star – 1943
Born 1921 in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia, Sarnai is one of the most famous and decorated pilot of the VVS (Voyenno-Vozdushnye Sily), the Sino-Soviet Union Military Air Forces. With her father being one of the rare pilots in her home country, Sarnai learned to fly at an early age. A natural talent, she started flying between her hometown and Irkutsk, carrying love letters, precious merchandises and shady characters. When her country officially joined the war effort against Axis powers, she then joined the newly formed Mongolian Arat Squadron, in 1943. Her impressive skills soon proved to be invaluable and she was transferred to the 46th “Taman” Guards Night Bomber Aviation Regiment (the “Night Witches”). Accumulating an impressive score of victories, she was tasked to form the 179th Guards Assault Aviation Regiment that she now commands. It was the first unit to receive the “Grom” (thunder in Russian)in the Red Air Force. The Mil Mi-47Z “Grom” Tesla Assault Helicopter is the latest addition to the already impressive arsenal of the VVS. Quickly catching up with their German counterparts, the Soviet engineers have miniaturized their VK generator enough so they can fit inside the tight fuselage of an attack helicopter. This gives the aircraft a tremendous firepower, almost without equal on the planet. It takes incredible piloting skills to hit anything with Tesla guns from the air, but the results are devastating: the whole enemy aircraft shuts down, its controls (or pilot...) fried.

This reference comes with three Dust 1947 unit cards. Like all regular Dust 1947 miniatures, it comes already assembled and primed. It's ready for the battlefield, or for painting, as you wish! You can directly paint over on it, since the priming serves as an undercoat and is compatible with any type of paint.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Mansions of Madness: Second Edition – Path of the Serpent: Expansion
description:
Now in Mansions of Madness, you take on the role of Arkham’s bravest investigators who must step into the unexplored wilderness of the Amazon jungle and explore crumbling ruins, discover the secrets of lost civilizations, and stop a serpent’s curse before the world descends into chaos. In three new scenarios, you will battle horrifying serpent creatures, face stone monstrosities that come to life, and beat the ever-present danger of being lost to the jungle forever.

Four new characters, four new monsters.

The scenarios:
In The Jungle Awakens - a lavish Arkham garden party shows off the host's recent acquisitions from a South American expedition.

Into the Dark - your team of investigators have been hired to assist a South American expedition.

Lost Temple of Yig - takes your investigators to ancient stone halls where serpentine priests attempt to reforge their broken bonds with their god, Yig.

The new characters:
Leo Anderson - able to help his allies cut through the jungle underbrush and allows them to move one space when he performs a move action.

Ursula Downs, the explorer. Once per round, she may listen to the call of the unknown and choose to move one space before or after exploring or interacting with a sight token.

Daniela Reyes, the mechanic - whenever she or one of her comrades solves a puzzle during an adventure, she may either gain two clues or discard one horror.

Norman Withers, Miskatonic Astronomer - May gain two clues or discard one horror once per round, provided he is in an outdoor space where he can read what is written in the stars.
family: Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

boardgame: Mystery Detective Vol. 2: Funny Death and Real Life Cases
description:
Mystery Detective is a mystery solving party game that you can start playing almost as soon as you open the box.

One player, the Chief, takes one of the 100 illustrated cards and reads it to himself. The front of the card has the mystery, the back the solution. Then he reads the front of the card to the players and shows them the illustration. The other players are the detectives. Singly and together, they try to solve the mystery by asking the Chief questions. But he can only answer Yes, No, Close, or Not Relevant.

Here is a mystery:
Title: Nasty Finish
Excessive hurry led a man to a stinky situation.
The picture on the card shows a man about to step into it.

What’s going on?

The detectives discuss and try to form theories that can be proved or disproved by their questions:
Is the man afraid? - no
Did the man die? - yes
Was he murdered?- no

So it must have been an accident…

Now we're getting somewhere…

Bit by bit the detectives try to piece together the mystery. Often a question from one detective will give another an idea for a whole new line of questioning. There is no winner or loser but when you finally solve the mystery, you feel brilliant!

The mysteries, while not all crimes, are morbid and twisted and often very funny. You need to discuss, question and think outside of the box in order to solve them. Great fun, you can play almost anywhere, anytime. In the car, at the dinner table or at a gathering, all are perfect for this co-cop mystery solving game.

This volume contains 100 cases. 
For Black Stories fans, this second volume includes never before published in English, translations of Black Stories Funny Death and Black Stories Shit Happens.

- description by the publisher

Mystery Detective is a mystery solving party game that you can start playing almost as soon as you open the box.

One player, the Chief, takes one of the 100 illustrated cards and reads it to himself. The front of the card has the mystery, the back the solution. Then he reads the front of the card to the players and shows them the illustration. The other players are the detectives. Singly and together, they try to solve the mystery by asking the Chief questions. But he can only answer Yes, No, Close, or Not Relevant.

Here is the first mystery:
A woman dies because she was on the phone too long.

We often talk too long on the phone but don't die! What's going on?

The detectives discuss and try to form theories that can be proved or disproved by their questions:
A lovers' quarrel?
Was the woman killed by her husband? - no
By her lover?- no

Okay, maybe international espionage:
Was the woman a spy? - no, not relevant

Hmm. Maybe we can localize this.
Was the woman lying down?- no
Standing up? - no
Sitting?  - YES!!

Now we're getting somewhere…

Bit by bit the detectives try to piece together the mystery. Often a question from one detective will give another an idea for a whole new line of questioning. There is no winner or loser but when you finally solve the mystery, you feel brilliant!

The mysteries, while not all crimes, are morbid and twisted and often very funny. You need to discuss, question and think outside of the box in order to solve them. Great fun, you can play almost anywhere, anytime. In the car, at the dinner table or at a gathering, all are perfect for this co-cop mystery solving game.

This volume contains 100 cases. 
For Black Stories fans, this first volume includes new translations of Black Stories 1 and 2.

-description from the publisher

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Wassermagierin Heldentableau
description:
Wassermagierin Heldentableau is an expansion for Legends of Andor and was offered through various contests on the Andor website.  This board is also available for download on the German Legenden-von-Andor.de website.

Die Wassermagier aus dem Land Danwar waren mächtige Wesen, die die Kräfte des Wassers für ihre eigenen Zwecke nutzten, indem sie eins mit dem Wasser wurden.

The water mages from Danwar are powerful beings who use the powers of water for their own purposes by becoming one with the water.

With this hero board, the The Legends of Andor is expanded with an additional strong heroine and an equally strong hero. Jarid and Jarim will replace the warrior Mairen or Thorn and will be played using the blue dice, disks and cube. As a special ability this hero can travel from one unused fountain to another. The willpower from these fountains (6 WP) can be distributed amongst the other heroes. Jarid and Jarim must leave behind gold, gemstones, objects, wood, pawns, or other figures when traveling between wells.

2019 Summer One Page PnP Contest Entry

(contains references to drinking; if that bothers you the game is probably not for you)

The end game is simple - end up with more fingers left on your hand than your opponent.  Battle against one opponent to drink your spirits the fastest by rolling dice and playing cards.  Simple, easy-to-learn game play; very transportable to take wherever.

—description from the designer

An area control game that gradually raises the stakes.

Cards are arranged in a line from the least valuable to the most valuable. On their turn a player must either claim a card, place a marker on the least valuable occupied card or least valued unoccupied card, or move an existing marker equal to the number of markers on their current card. If they move off the end of the line however, their marker is lost without scoring. When all of the cards have been removed or claimed, the player with the most valuable collection wins.

This is a print and play game. In addition you will need 8 markers per player.

—description from the designer

Fall of Ming: Jisi Incident is low-complexity wargame on the Jisi incident during ancient China. This is a 2 players wargame. One player control the army of Qing dynasty and one player control the army of Ming Dynasty.

The goal for army of Qing dynasty was to occupy the cities of Hebei Province.

—description from the designer
